GHOST Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Requirements
Deep Traceability
  • VIEWS
  • Document
  • Table
  • Traceability
  • Deep Traceability

    • 1. 1. Purpose and Scope
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
      2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
    1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

    The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

    The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME
      1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME The product described by this document shall be named GHOST.

      The existing Python package namespace may remain ragstream in current source code and implementation paths.

      Requirements shall not treat ragstream as the product identity.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL
      1.3. Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL The GHOST prompt-preparation capability shall be organized into these three functional parts:

      1. Deterministic Prompt Structuring.
      2. ActiveBrief Relation Classification and Routing.
      3. A2 PromptShaper and Job Structuring.

      Each part shall have explicit inputs, outputs, responsibility boundaries, and acceptance criteria.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE
      1.4. Current Executable Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE The current executable scope shall include:
      - schema-driven deterministic header parsing and name resolution;
      - TASK and CONTEXT fallback behavior;
      - default handling for active mandatory fields;
      - SuperPrompt body and lifecycle updates;
      - ActiveBrief discovery and snapshotting;
      - two-dimensional LLM classification of prompt materiality and topic relation;
      - deterministic routing policy derived from the classifier result;
      - A2 selector execution or deterministic-default mode;
      - synchronized effective retrieval-query creation;
      - central SuperPrompt projection for the prepared prompt preview.

      Unimplemented semantic header matching, fuzzy matching, future A2 passes, and downstream retrieval algorithms shall not be presented as current behavior.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW
      2.1. Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW The prompt-preparation capability shall accept raw user text and shall produce a SuperPrompt whose current request, meta-configuration, ActiveBrief routing metadata, effective retrieval query, lifecycle state, and prompt preview are ready for downstream context generation.

      The output of this capability shall be the authoritative prepared runtime state for the current request.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY
        2.2. Deterministic Preprocessing Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY Header parsing, key normalization, canonical-name matching, alias matching, bilingual matching, TASK/CONTEXT fallback, active mandatory-field defaulting, body write-back, and lifecycle bookkeeping shall be deterministic.

        The deterministic preprocessing layer shall not call an LLM and shall not apply fuzzy, semantic, template, cue-based, or probabilistic header inference in the current runtime.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY
        2.3. ActiveBrief Classifier Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY The ActiveBrief relation classifier shall decide only:
        - prompt_materiality; and
        - topic_relation.

        It shall not decide document-retrieval actions, memory-context actions, final prompt-display actions, pipeline stage order, or downstream evidence behavior.

        Those actions shall be determined by explicit deterministic Python policy.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY
        2.4. A2 PromptShaper Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY A2 PromptShaper shall classify configured prompt meta-fields from the already normalized current request.

        A2 shall not parse raw Markdown headers, select an ActiveBrief, execute Knowledge Retrieval, execute Memory Retrieval, or own final answer generation.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE
        2.5. Agent Stack Infrastructure Reference GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE ActiveBrief relation classification and A2 PromptShaper shall use the neutral Agent Stack contracts defined in 20_agent_stack.sdoc.

        This file shall own the functional meaning, runtime payload, deterministic validation, routing policy, and SuperPrompt write-back of these concrete prompt functions.

        This file shall not duplicate the generic AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, or LLMClient infrastructure requirements.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY
        2.6. SuperPrompt State Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY SuperPrompt shall remain the authoritative shared runtime state for prompt preparation.

        PromptSchema, NameMatcher, ActiveBrief classifier configuration, AgentPrompt, and LLMClient shall not become parallel owners of the current prepared prompt state.

        All persistent prompt-preparation outcomes required by downstream stages shall be written explicitly into SuperPrompt body fields, lifecycle fields, or extras.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY
        2.7. Controller Coordination Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY The application controller shall coordinate deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, central prompt projection, and A2 invocation.

        The controller shall not absorb schema normalization rules, classifier semantics, A2 selector sanitization, or downstream retrieval algorithms.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY
        2.8. Knowledge Management Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY This file shall prepare the effective document-retrieval query and document-retrieval routing state.

        Detailed document ingestion, dense retrieval, SPLADE retrieval, fusion, reranking, usefulness selection, and evidence condensation shall remain owned by the Knowledge Management requirement files.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY
        2.9. Memory Management Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY This file shall prepare the memory-context permission and ActiveBrief continuity state required by downstream memory processing.

        Detailed memory recording, ActiveBrief creation and update, memory ingestion, memory retrieval, memory scoring, and memory-context synthesis shall remain owned by the Memory Management requirement files.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
      3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

      The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON
        3.2. Prompt Schema JSON Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON The current prompt schema shall be represented as a JSON object.

        The executable deterministic preprocessing contract may use:
        - canonical_keys;
        - must_keys;
        - defaults;
        - aliases; and
        - bilingual mappings.

        Additional schema sections shall not be treated as active runtime behavior unless a current requirement and implementation consume them.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION
        3.3. Header-Key Normalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Prompt header keys shall be normalized deterministically by:
        1. accepting string input only;
        2. applying Unicode NFKC normalization;
        3. converting to lowercase;
        4. trimming leading and trailing whitespace;
        5. collapsing internal whitespace runs; and
        6. removing punctuation from the header key used for matching.

        Header-key normalization shall not modify the section body text.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION
          12.6. AC: Header-Key Normalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION Given two header names that differ only by case, Unicode compatibility form, outer whitespace, internal whitespace runs, or punctuation removed by the normalization contract, they shall resolve to the same normalized matching key.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS
        3.4. Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS The current SuperPrompt prompt-body contract shall support these runtime keys:
        - system;
        - task;
        - audience;
        - role;
        - tone;
        - depth;
        - confidence;
        - evidence_policy;
        - context;
        - purpose;
        - format;
        - text.

        Prompt preprocessing shall write only to fields defined by the active SuperPrompt body contract.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING
          3.5. Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-facing canonical names shall have an explicit deterministic mapping to the active SuperPrompt runtime keys.

          In particular:
          - response_depth shall map to depth;
          - output_format shall map to format.

          A schema key shall not be considered successfully applied merely because it was recognized if no runtime destination receives its value.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION
            3.6. Mandatory Field Runtime Destination GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION Every active schema field declared mandatory shall have one defined runtime destination or shall be removed from the active mandatory-field set.

            The system shall not silently claim enforcement of a mandatory schema field whose value is discarded before SuperPrompt write-back.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS
              4.12. Other Mandatory Field Defaults GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS After TASK fallback, each other active mandatory prompt field that remains absent or empty shall receive its configured schema default.

              Defaults shall be applied only when the field has an active runtime destination.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP
            12.11. AC: Schema-to-Runtime Mapping GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP Given a recognized response_depth or output_format value, prompt preprocessing shall route it to runtime depth or format respectively.

            A configured mandatory field without a runtime destination shall fail requirement conformance rather than being reported as enforced.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
        3.7. Canonical Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
          3.8. Alias Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

          The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH
            3.9. Bilingual Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically or through an alias may resolve through a configured bilingual mapping.

            The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER
              3.10. Deterministic Name-Matching Order GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Header resolution shall use this fixed order:
              1. canonical match;
              2. alias match;
              3. bilingual match;
              4. unknown.

              A lower-priority method shall not override a successful higher-priority match.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING
                3.11. No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING The current deterministic header resolver shall not use edit distance, typo correction, templates, keyword cues, embeddings, LLM inference, or semantic similarity.

                Schema data for such future behavior may exist without making that behavior part of the current executable requirement.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER
              12.5. AC: Bilingual Header Resolution GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER Given a heading named Aufgabe and a configured German mapping from aufgabe to task, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime task field.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER
            12.4. AC: Alias Header Resolution GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER Given a heading named Objective and an alias mapping from objective to purpose, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime purpose field.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER
          12.3. AC: Canonical Header Resolution GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER Given a supported heading named TASK, the parser shall resolve it canonically and write its body into the runtime task field.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION
        3.12. Prompt Schema Version Traceability GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION The prompt schema shall expose a version identifier sufficient to trace the canonical field vocabulary used by a prompt-preparation baseline.

        An approved change to canonical keys, mandatory fields, defaults, aliases, bilingual mappings, or schema-to-runtime mappings shall update the affected requirements and verification assets.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
      4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

      Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
        4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

        Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX
          4.3. Current Header Syntax GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX A current prompt-field heading shall contain:
          - one to three leading hash characters;
          - optional whitespace after the hash characters; and
          - a non-empty header title.

          Therefore both "## TASK" and "##TASK" shall be interpreted according to the same current header-title matching rules.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY
          4.4. Section Body Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY The body of a recognized or unrecognized heading shall extend from the end of that heading to the beginning of the next supported heading or to the end of the raw input.

          Leading and trailing whitespace around each extracted body shall be removed.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
          4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS
            4.7. Repeated Canonical Field Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS When multiple headings resolve to the same canonical field, the body of the last occurrence shall replace the earlier occurrence for the current preprocessing run.

            The parser shall not concatenate repeated field bodies automatically.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS
              12.7. AC: Repeated Field Last Wins GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS Given two supported headings that both resolve to task, the body of the second occurrence shall become the final task value for that preprocessing run.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS
            4.8. Unknown Header Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS A header that cannot be resolved through canonical, alias, or bilingual matching shall not create an arbitrary SuperPrompt body field.

            Its body shall not overwrite a recognized field.

            The current prompt-preparation contract does not require fuzzy recovery of the unknown header.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER
              12.10. AC: Unknown Header Isolation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER Given an unrecognized heading, deterministic preprocessing shall not create a new SuperPrompt body key and shall not overwrite a recognized canonical field.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
            4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

            Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
              4.10. TASK from CONTEXT Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK If structured input contains no non-empty TASK but contains a non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK for the current request.

              The consumed CONTEXT shall then be cleared from the current prompt body so that the same text is not presented twice as both TASK and CONTEXT.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
                12.8. AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK Given structured input with no non-empty TASK and one non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK and the current runtime CONTEXT shall be cleared.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK
              4.11. TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK If structured input contains neither a non-empty TASK nor a non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw user prompt shall become TASK.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK
                12.9. AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK Given structured input with neither non-empty TASK nor non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw input shall become TASK.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION
            4.13. Prompt Body Text Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Deterministic header normalization and matching shall affect header names only.

            Recognized section body text shall preserve the user's wording except for outer whitespace trimming and the explicit TASK/CONTEXT fallback behavior.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE
        4.5. Plain-Text Prompt Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE When no supported heading is present, the complete trimmed raw user text shall become the TASK value.

        Plain-text mode shall not require schema name matching.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK
          12.2. AC: Plain Text Becomes TASK GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK Given raw input with no supported heading, deterministic preprocessing shall write the complete trimmed input into SuperPrompt.body["task"], set stage to preprocessed, append the preprocessing history entry, and preserve retrieval artifacts.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE
        12.1. AC: Whitespace-Only Input GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE Given whitespace-only raw input, the controller shall return the existing SuperPrompt without invoking deterministic preprocessing, relation classification, or A2 and without appending a new preprocessing history entry.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK
        5.2. Canonical Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Recognized and mapped prompt values shall be written into the corresponding active SuperPrompt body fields.

        The write-back shall reflect last-wins behavior, TASK fallback, CONTEXT consumption, and active mandatory-field defaults.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS
          5.3. No Dynamic Body-Field Creation GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS Deterministic preprocessing shall not add new keys outside the active SuperPrompt body contract.

          New canonical prompt fields shall require an approved SuperPrompt contract change and explicit schema-to-runtime mapping.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS
          5.4. Preservation of Untouched Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS A body field not recognized, defaulted, or explicitly cleared by the current preprocessing rules shall retain its existing value.

          Prompt preparation shall not clear retrieval artifacts or unrelated SuperPrompt fields merely because preprocessing is re-run.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION
        5.5. Initial Retrieval-Query Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION After deterministic body write-back, GHOST may build an initial retrieval-oriented text projection from TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT.

        This initial projection shall not be treated as final until ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing have completed.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK
        5.6. Preprocessed Stage Mark GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall set SuperPrompt.stage to "preprocessed".
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK
          5.7. Preprocessing History Entry GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall append "preprocessed" to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION
        5.8. No Retrieval Artifact Mutation GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION Prompt preparation before the retrieval handoff shall not create, rank, remove, or rewrite document chunks, memory candidates, stage ranking views, final document selections, condensed document context, or synthesized memory context.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW
        5.9. Deterministic Intermediate Preview GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW Deterministic preprocessing may create an intermediate prompt preview from the currently populated prompt fields.

        That intermediate preview shall not supersede the final centralized projection performed after relation classification and A2 shaping.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CENTRAL-PROJECTION
          5.10. Central Prompt Projection after Preparation GHOST-PREPROCESS-CENTRAL-PROJECTION At the completion of the composite preprocessing operation, the controller shall invoke the central SuperPrompt projection path so that prompt_ready reflects the current body fields, evidence policy, ActiveBrief routing state, and current lifecycle state.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL
      6.1. Optional ActiveBrief Input GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Prompt preparation shall support execution with or without a configured memory manager and with or without an eligible ActiveBrief.

      Absence of an ActiveBrief shall be a valid routing state and shall not be treated as an LLM failure.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-ELIGIBILITY
        6.2. Current Eligible ActiveBrief GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-ELIGIBILITY When a memory manager is available, the classifier input shall use the latest eligible memory record, searched from newest to oldest, that:
        - is not tagged Black; and
        - contains a non-empty ActiveRetrievalBrief body.

        The semantic meaning and lifecycle of memory tags and ActiveRetrievalBrief persistence shall remain owned by the Memory Management requirements.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-SNAPSHOT
          6.3. ActiveBrief Snapshot GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-SNAPSHOT The relation-classification result shall preserve a snapshot containing the selected ActiveBrief record identifier, title, and body.

          If no eligible ActiveBrief exists, the snapshot shall contain empty values rather than an invented brief.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-CURRENT-PROMPT
          6.5. Classifier Current-Prompt Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-CURRENT-PROMPT When an eligible ActiveBrief exists, the relation classifier shall receive the normalized current prompt built from available PURPOSE, TASK, and CONTEXT values.

          The current prompt shall remain distinguishable from the ActiveBrief title and body.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-RAW-FALLBACK
            6.6. Classifier Raw-Text Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-RAW-FALLBACK If the normalized PURPOSE, TASK, and CONTEXT projection is empty, the classifier input builder may use the trimmed raw user text as the current prompt.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-CURRENT-BYPASS
              6.7. Empty Current-Prompt Bypass GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-CURRENT-BYPASS If an eligible ActiveBrief exists but the classifier current-prompt text remains empty, GHOST shall not invoke the LLM.

              It shall deterministically record:
              - prompt_materiality = WEAK;
              - topic_relation = IRRELEVANT;
              - relation classifier LLM used = false.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-EMPTY-CURRENT
                12.13. AC: Empty Current Prompt with ActiveBrief GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-EMPTY-CURRENT Given an eligible ActiveBrief but no usable current-prompt text, relation classification shall not call the LLM and shall write WEAK + IRRELEVANT with memory context disabled and final_prompt_mode none.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OWNERSHIP
          6.8. ActiveBrief Durable-Truth Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OWNERSHIP Prompt preparation shall read the current eligible ActiveBrief for routing but shall not create, update, overwrite, persist, or become the durable owner of the ActiveRetrievalBrief.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF-BYPASS
        6.4. No-ActiveBrief Classifier Bypass GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF-BYPASS When no eligible ActiveBrief exists, GHOST shall not invoke the relation-classifier LLM.

        It shall record:
        - prompt_materiality = UNKNOWN;
        - topic_relation = NO_ACTIVEBRIEF;
        - relation classifier LLM used = false.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION
      7.1. Two Independent Classification Dimensions GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION The relation classifier shall return exactly two semantic decisions:
      - prompt_materiality; and
      - topic_relation.

      The classifier shall not collapse both dimensions into one combined LLM label.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATERIALITY-CONTRACT
        7.2. Prompt Materiality Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATERIALITY-CONTRACT Prompt materiality shall be classified from the current prompt alone.

        Allowed values shall be:
        - STRONG: the current prompt independently identifies a task, topic, or information need;
        - WEAK: the current prompt depends mainly on previous conversational context.

        Relation to the ActiveBrief shall not by itself make a prompt STRONG.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CONTRACT
        7.3. Topic Relation Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CONTRACT Topic relation shall be classified using both the current prompt and the ActiveBrief.

        Allowed values shall be:
        - SAME_TOPIC: direct continuation or clear reference to the current ActiveBrief topic;
        - RELATED_DOMAIN: a different immediate topic within the same broader professional or technical domain;
        - IRRELEVANT: another domain or no useful contextual bridge.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFICATION-ORDER
        7.4. Classification Decision Order GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFICATION-ORDER The classifier instruction shall require prompt materiality to be decided first from the current prompt alone and topic relation to be decided second using the current prompt and ActiveBrief.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-CONFIG
        7.5. Configured Relation Classifier GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-CONFIG The current relation classifier shall be represented by a versioned Agent Stack configuration defining:
        - classifier identity;
        - model settings;
        - static classifier rules;
        - ActiveBrief and current-prompt bindings;
        - the two decision targets; and
        - the structured output mapping.

        The functional behavior in this file shall remain independent of the physical JSON path.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
          7.6. Exact Classifier Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT The relation-classifier LLM shall return one JSON object containing exactly:
          - prompt_materiality; and
          - topic_relation.

          It shall not return action decisions, explanations, prose, or additional keys as part of the required output contract.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-VALIDATION
            7.7. Classifier Result Validation GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-VALIDATION After AgentPrompt parsing, GHOST shall validate that prompt_materiality and topic_relation belong to their exact allowed value sets.

            An invalid or unknown value shall fail explicitly and shall not be converted into a fabricated valid routing state.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CLASSIFIER-VALUES
              12.14. AC: Exact Relation Classifier Values GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CLASSIFIER-VALUES Given a normal relation-classifier call, the accepted parsed result shall contain one allowed prompt_materiality value and one allowed topic_relation value.

              Any other value shall fail explicitly before routing action fields are written as a valid decision.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RELATION-STATE
        7.8. Combined Relation State GHOST-PREPROCESS-RELATION-STATE For an eligible ActiveBrief, GHOST shall derive a combined relation-state identifier from prompt_materiality and topic_relation.

        For absence of an ActiveBrief, the relation state shall be NO_ACTIVEBRIEF.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT
        7.9. Deterministic Routing Decision Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT GHOST shall deterministically derive one routing decision containing:
        - prompt_materiality;
        - topic_relation;
        - use_activebrief_for_retrieval;
        - final_prompt_mode;
        - allow_memory_context; and
        - memory_context_policy.

        The LLM shall not populate these action fields directly.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF
          7.10. Routing State: No ActiveBrief GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF For NO_ACTIVEBRIEF, GHOST shall set:
          - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = false;
          - final_prompt_mode = none;
          - allow_memory_context = true;
          - memory_context_policy = normal_no_activebrief.

          The effective document query shall use the current prompt only.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF
            12.12. AC: No ActiveBrief State GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF Given no eligible ActiveBrief, relation classification shall not call the LLM and shall write UNKNOWN, NO_ACTIVEBRIEF, an empty ActiveBrief snapshot, and the normal_no_activebrief routing decision.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-STRONG-SAME
          7.11. Routing State: Strong and Same Topic GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-STRONG-SAME For STRONG + SAME_TOPIC, GHOST shall set:
          - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = false;
          - final_prompt_mode = full_activebrief;
          - allow_memory_context = true;
          - memory_context_policy = allowed.

          The effective document query shall use the current prompt without adding the ActiveBrief.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-SAME
          7.12. Routing State: Weak and Same Topic GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-SAME For WEAK + SAME_TOPIC, GHOST shall set:
          - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = true;
          - final_prompt_mode = full_activebrief;
          - allow_memory_context = true;
          - memory_context_policy = allowed.

          The effective document query shall contain the current prompt and the full ActiveBrief body.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-SAME-QUERY
            12.16. AC: Weak Same-Topic Retrieval Query GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-SAME-QUERY Given WEAK + SAME_TOPIC and a non-empty ActiveBrief body, the finalized effective retrieval query shall include the current TASK/PURPOSE/CONTEXT projection and the full ActiveBrief body.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-STRONG-RELATED
          7.13. Routing State: Strong and Related Domain GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-STRONG-RELATED For STRONG + RELATED_DOMAIN, GHOST shall set:
          - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = false;
          - final_prompt_mode = title_related_domain_note;
          - allow_memory_context = true;
          - memory_context_policy = allowed_related_domain.

          The effective document query shall use the current prompt without adding the ActiveBrief.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-RELATED
          7.14. Routing State: Weak and Related Domain GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-RELATED For WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN, GHOST shall set:
          - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = true;
          - final_prompt_mode = title_related_domain_note;
          - allow_memory_context = true;
          - memory_context_policy = allowed_related_domain.

          The effective document query shall contain the current prompt and the ActiveBrief title, but not the full ActiveBrief body.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-RELATED-QUERY
            12.17. AC: Weak Related-Domain Retrieval Query GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-RELATED-QUERY Given WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN and a non-empty ActiveBrief title, the finalized effective retrieval query shall include the current TASK/PURPOSE/CONTEXT projection and the ActiveBrief title without the full ActiveBrief body.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-STRONG-IRRELEVANT
          7.15. Routing State: Strong and Irrelevant GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-STRONG-IRRELEVANT For STRONG + IRRELEVANT, GHOST shall set:
          - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = false;
          - final_prompt_mode = title_topic_shift_note;
          - allow_memory_context = false;
          - memory_context_policy = disabled.

          The effective document query shall use the current prompt without adding the ActiveBrief.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-IRRELEVANT
          7.16. Routing State: Weak and Irrelevant GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-IRRELEVANT For WEAK + IRRELEVANT, GHOST shall set:
          - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = false;
          - final_prompt_mode = none;
          - allow_memory_context = false;
          - memory_context_policy = disabled.

          The effective document-retrieval query shall be empty, signaling that document retrieval is not applicable for this state.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-IRRELEVANT-QUERY
            12.18. AC: Weak Irrelevant Retrieval Suppression GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-IRRELEVANT-QUERY Given WEAK + IRRELEVANT, the finalized effective document-retrieval query shall be empty and memory context shall be disabled.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-FINAL-MODE-AUTHORITY
          7.17. Final Prompt Mode Authority GHOST-PREPROCESS-FINAL-MODE-AUTHORITY Central prompt projection shall honor the deterministic final_prompt_mode stored by prompt preparation.

          The projector shall not independently derive an ActiveBrief inclusion behavior that contradicts the routing decision.

          The supported current modes shall mean:
          - full_activebrief: include title and full brief as supporting continuity context;
          - title_related_domain_note: include the title with related-domain continuity framing;
          - title_topic_shift_note: include the title only for explicit topic-shift awareness;
          - none: include no ActiveBrief content.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-FINAL-MODE
            12.20. AC: Final Prompt Mode Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-FINAL-MODE Given a deterministic final_prompt_mode, the central projector shall include exactly the permitted ActiveBrief continuity material for that mode and shall not independently apply a contradictory state table.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-POLICY-AUTHORITY
          7.18. Memory Context Permission Authority GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-POLICY-AUTHORITY Downstream Memory Management shall honor allow_memory_context and memory_context_policy from the deterministic routing decision.

          A disabled memory-context state shall not be overridden by generic memory retrieval merely because memory data is available.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROUTING-EXTRAS
          7.19. Relation and Routing Metadata in SuperPrompt GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROUTING-EXTRAS Prompt preparation shall expose the relation-classification and routing result through stable SuperPrompt extras sufficient for downstream traceability.

          The current contract shall include:
          - activebrief_prompt_materiality;
          - activebrief_topic_relation;
          - activebrief_relation_state;
          - activebrief_relation_llm_used;
          - activebrief_relation_decision;
          - activebrief_relation_activebrief.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROUTING-LOG
          7.20. Public Relation Summary GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROUTING-LOG Prompt preparation shall emit a compact user-visible summary of the current topic relation and prompt strength after the routing result is written.

          Detailed prompts, ActiveBrief bodies, or raw model output shall not be exposed through the public log solely to explain the classification.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SIX-STATE-MATRIX
          12.15. AC: Six-State Deterministic Routing Matrix GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SIX-STATE-MATRIX Given each valid STRONG/WEAK and SAME_TOPIC/RELATED_DOMAIN/IRRELEVANT combination, GHOST shall produce exactly the routing values defined in the corresponding state requirement.

          The same classification pair shall always produce the same routing decision.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
      8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

      Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
        8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

        The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS
          8.3. A2 Decision Targets GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS The current A2 PromptShaper shall classify these six fields:
          - system;
          - audience;
          - tone;
          - depth;
          - confidence;
          - evidence_policy.

          System may select one to three configured role identifiers. Each other field shall select exactly one configured identifier.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
          8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
          1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
          2. compose the selector messages;
          3. call the configured LLM path;
          4. parse the structured result; and
          5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
            8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

            The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL
              8.9. A2 Invalid Identifier Removal GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL For each A2 field, deterministic sanitization shall remove option identifiers that are not present in that field's allowed catalog.

              This rule shall remove invented identifiers, malformed identifiers, and identifiers copied from another field's catalog.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID
                12.21. AC: A2 Invalid Identifier Sanitization GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID Given an A2 result containing an invented identifier or an identifier from another field's catalog, the sanitizer shall remove that identifier before SuperPrompt write-back.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL
              8.10. A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL Deterministic A2 sanitization shall remove duplicate option identifiers while preserving the first valid occurrence order.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY
              8.11. A2 Cardinality Enforcement GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY A2 sanitization shall enforce each active field's configured maximum selection count.

              When more valid identifiers are returned than allowed, the first valid identifiers up to the configured maximum shall be retained.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE
              8.12. Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE If an A2 field has no valid identifier after sanitization, A2 shall omit that field from write-back.

              A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value and shall not apply a catalog default as semantic repair after invalid LLM output.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE
                12.22. AC: A2 Empty Sanitized Field Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE Given an A2 field whose candidate output becomes empty after sanitization, A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value by omitting that field from write-back.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
              8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

              When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

              For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS
                8.14. A2 Diagnostic Metadata GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS A2 shall write stable runtime metadata sufficient to identify:
                - the sanitized selected identifiers;
                - whether the LLM was used; and
                - whether execution used LLM mode or deterministic-default mode.

                Current keys shall include a2_selected_ids, a2_llm_used, and a2_mode.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
                8.15. A2 Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

                A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
                • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE
                  8.16. A2 Stage Mark GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE After successful A2 processing, SuperPrompt.stage shall be set to "a2".
                  • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY
                    8.17. A2 History Entry GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY After successful A2 processing, "a2" shall be appended to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
                  • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE
                    12.25. AC: A2 Lifecycle and Artifact Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE After successful A2 execution, stage shall equal a2, history shall contain the new a2 entry, prompt_ready shall reflect the A2 selections, and existing document or memory retrieval artifacts shall remain unchanged.
                • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION
                  8.18. A2 Prompt Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION After A2 write-back, GHOST shall rebuild prompt_ready through the current central SuperPrompt composition contract so that selected meta-field labels and evidence policy are visible in the prepared prompt preview.
                • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION
                  8.19. A2 Retrieval-State Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 shall not create, remove, reorder, or rewrite document retrieval results, memory retrieval results, evidence selections, condensed context, or retrieval-stage score views.
                • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN
                  8.20. Safe A2 Re-Execution GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN The orchestrator may re-run A2 after preprocessing when meta-field refinement is required.

                  Re-running A2 shall apply the same sanitization and write-back rules and shall not clear existing retrieval artifacts solely because the lifecycle stage returns to "a2".
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL
                12.24. AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL Given valid sanitized A2 identifiers with configured labels, SuperPrompt extras shall retain the identifiers while the body fields contain their human-readable labels.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE
          8.6. A2 Deterministic-Default Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE When A2 LLM mode is disabled, A2 shall construct its candidate selector result deterministically from configured defaults.

          If a configured default is absent, A2 may use the first allowed configured option or the allowed prefix required by a multi-selection target.

          The same deterministic sanitization and write-back rules shall apply after candidate construction.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT
            12.23. AC: A2 Deterministic-Default Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT Given use_llm = false and valid configured defaults, A2 shall produce a sanitized selector result without an LLM call, mark a2_llm_used false, and mark its mode as deterministic_defaults.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS
          8.7. A2 Configured Model Settings GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS A2 shall use model name, temperature where applicable, and maximum output-token allowance obtained from its AgentPrompt configuration.

          A2 business logic shall not silently replace configured model settings with unrelated hard-coded values.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD
        8.4. A2 Runtime Payload GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD A2 shall build its prompt-under-evaluation from the available PURPOSE, TASK, and CONTEXT values of the current SuperPrompt.

        A2 shall also provide an explicit required-output JSON shape derived from the active configured decision targets.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE
        9.2. Retrieval Query Source Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE The current prompt portion of the effective retrieval query shall be derived from available TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT values.

        A2 meta-fields such as system, audience, tone, depth, confidence, and evidence_policy shall not be inserted into the document-retrieval query merely because they appear in the final prompt preview.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF
        9.3. ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief content shall contribute to the effective retrieval query only according to the deterministic routing state:
        - WEAK + SAME_TOPIC: full ActiveBrief body;
        - WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN: ActiveBrief title;
        - all STRONG states: no ActiveBrief contribution;
        - WEAK + IRRELEVANT: empty query;
        - NO_ACTIVEBRIEF: current prompt only.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF
        9.4. Knowledge Retrieval Handoff GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF The Knowledge Management path shall receive:
        - the prepared SuperPrompt;
        - the synchronized effective retrieval-query text; and
        - routing state sufficient to determine whether document retrieval is applicable.

        This handoff shall not precompute document candidates.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION
          9.8. No Knowledge Retrieval Execution GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION The prompt-preparation capability shall not query ChromaDB, SPLADE indexes, document manifests, rerankers, A3, or A4.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL
            12.27. AC: No Retrieval inside Prompt Preparation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL Executing the complete prompt-preparation capability shall not query document or memory vector stores and shall not run reranking, A3, A4, or memory synthesis.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF
        9.5. Memory Retrieval Handoff GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF The Memory Management path shall receive:
        - the prepared SuperPrompt;
        - the ActiveBrief snapshot and relation state;
        - allow_memory_context; and
        - memory_context_policy.

        This handoff shall not precompute or synthesize memory context.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION
          9.9. No Memory Retrieval Execution GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION The prompt-preparation capability shall not query memory vector stores, run memory candidate scoring, merge memory records, or synthesize final memory context.

          Reading the current eligible ActiveBrief for relation classification shall not be treated as full Memory Retrieval execution.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL
        9.6. Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL An empty effective document-retrieval query produced by WEAK + IRRELEVANT routing shall be treated as an explicit document-retrieval suppression signal.

        Downstream orchestration shall skip document retrieval or handle the state without attempting a normal retrieval call with an empty query.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY
        9.7. Prepared-State Consistency GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY At handoff, the following shall describe the same current request and routing decision:
        - SuperPrompt body fields;
        - ActiveBrief relation extras;
        - deterministic routing decision;
        - effective retrieval-query text;
        - A2 selected identifiers and display labels;
        - lifecycle stage and history;
        - prompt_ready.

        Contradictory representations shall be treated as a defect.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY
          9.10. Current Request Priority in Prepared Prompt GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY The prepared prompt preview shall present the current user request as the highest-priority task.

          Any ActiveBrief continuity material shall be presented only as supporting context according to final_prompt_mode.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF
          12.26. AC: Prepared Retrieval Handoff GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF At completion of prompt preparation, the SuperPrompt body, routing extras, effective retrieval query, A2 metadata, stage/history, and prompt_ready shall be mutually consistent and sufficient for Knowledge and Memory Retrieval to begin without reparsing the raw user prompt.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD
        12.19. AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD Given an initial pre-classification query and a final routing state that changes ActiveBrief contribution or suppresses retrieval, the effective retrieval-query field shall be rebuilt after classification and shall reflect the final state at handoff.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR
      10.1. Prompt Schema Failure GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR A missing, unreadable, malformed, or non-object prompt schema shall fail explicitly during schema initialization.

      GHOST shall not silently continue with an invented schema when deterministic preprocessing depends on that configuration.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR
      10.2. Relation Classifier Failure Integrity GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR A provider failure, parse failure, missing required binding, or invalid classifier value shall not produce a fabricated STRONG, WEAK, SAME_TOPIC, RELATED_DOMAIN, or IRRELEVANT result.

      Fallback behavior, if introduced later, shall require an explicit approved requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR
      10.3. A2 Failure Integrity GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 provider or parse failure shall not be represented as a successful LLM selection.

      The deterministic-default mode shall be used only when explicitly selected by the caller, not as an undisclosed replacement for a failed LLM call.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL
      10.4. No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL GHOST shall not invoke the relation-classifier LLM when no eligible ActiveBrief exists or when the classifier current-prompt text is empty.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY
      10.5. Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt preparation shall emit compact public lifecycle and relation summaries and may emit richer internal or developer diagnostics according to the logging policy.

      Raw prompt payloads, ActiveBrief bodies, model messages, and raw model outputs shall not be exposed through public logs by default.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY
      10.6. Deterministic Reproducibility GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Given the same raw prompt, prompt schema, starting relevant SuperPrompt body state, and no LLM-enabled step, deterministic preprocessing shall produce the same recognized field values and routing-independent intermediate query text.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY
      10.7. Prompt Function Testability GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt parsing, name resolution, schema-to-runtime mapping, TASK fallback, routing-table construction, query projection, A2 sanitization, and SuperPrompt write-back shall be testable independently of the GUI.

      LLM-enabled classifier and A2 tests shall support provider stubs or recorded structured responses so deterministic post-processing can be verified.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS
      10.8. No Hidden Prompt Heuristics GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS Current prompt-preparation behavior shall not depend on undocumented fuzzy matching, semantic thresholds, hidden prompt repairs, or implicit ActiveBrief inclusion rules.

      Every current deterministic action shall be traceable to an explicit requirement and testable rule.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS
      11.1. Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt-preparation requirements shall remain traceable to the modules and configurations that implement schema loading, deterministic parsing, name matching, ActiveBrief relation classification, A2 shaping, SuperPrompt mutation, query projection, and controller coordination.

      A private helper shall not become the owner of a system requirement merely because it currently contains the implementing function.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION
        11.3. Requirement and Implementation Synchronization GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION An approved change to prompt schema semantics, deterministic parsing, canonical field mapping, ActiveBrief routing, A2 targets, query projection, public interfaces, or SuperPrompt write-back shall update the affected requirements and verification assets together with the implementation change.

        Private refactoring that preserves required behavior and interfaces shall not require new functional requirements.

        Code that contradicts an approved requirement in this file shall be treated as a defect or unapproved change until the contradiction is resolved.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION
          12.28. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION An implementation baseline shall not be accepted when its prompt-schema mapping, routing table, query projection, final prompt projection, or A2 sanitization contradicts an approved requirement in this file.

          The contradiction shall be resolved by correcting the defect or approving a coordinated requirement and implementation change.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP
      11.2. Sibling Requirement Ownership GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Detailed behavior shall remain in the following sibling requirement areas:

      - neutral AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, and LLMClient infrastructure in 20_agent_stack.sdoc;
      - runtime stage coordination in 10_orchestrator_and_pipeline.sdoc;
      - document ingestion in 41_document_ingestion.sdoc;
      - document retrieval and reranking in 42_document_retrieval_and_reranking.sdoc;
      - document usefulness selection and condensation in 43_evidence_selection_and_condensation.sdoc;
      - memory recording and ActiveBrief creation/update in 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc;
      - memory ingestion and retrieval in 52_memory_ingestion_and_retrieval.sdoc;
      - memory merge, ActiveBrief continuity, and Blackboard direction in 53_memory_merge_activebrief_blackboard.sdoc;
      - current Streamlit controls and preview rendering in 61_streamlit_workbench_current.sdoc;
      - logging in 81_logging_textforge_raglog.sdoc;
      - security and traceability governance in 83_quality_security_traceability_governance.sdoc.

      This file shall not duplicate those subsystem algorithms.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY
      11.4. Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY The current requirement baseline shall not present the following as implemented unless corresponding code and tests exist:
      - fuzzy or edit-distance header matching;
      - semantic or LLM-based header recovery;
      - template or keyword-cue field inference;
      - automatic multi-pass A2 refinement;
      - new prompt body fields without SuperPrompt support;
      - new relation labels or routing states;
      - retrieval or memory algorithms inside preprocessing.

      Such capabilities shall be introduced through explicit future requirements and controlled implementation changes.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME
    1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRODUCT-NAME The product described by this document shall be named GHOST.

    The existing Python package namespace may remain ragstream in current source code and implementation paths.

    Requirements shall not treat ragstream as the product identity.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL
    1.3. Three-Part Prompt-Preparation Model GHOST-PREPROCESS-THREE-PART-MODEL The GHOST prompt-preparation capability shall be organized into these three functional parts:

    1. Deterministic Prompt Structuring.
    2. ActiveBrief Relation Classification and Routing.
    3. A2 PromptShaper and Job Structuring.

    Each part shall have explicit inputs, outputs, responsibility boundaries, and acceptance criteria.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE
    1.4. Current Executable Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-SCOPE The current executable scope shall include:
    - schema-driven deterministic header parsing and name resolution;
    - TASK and CONTEXT fallback behavior;
    - default handling for active mandatory fields;
    - SuperPrompt body and lifecycle updates;
    - ActiveBrief discovery and snapshotting;
    - two-dimensional LLM classification of prompt materiality and topic relation;
    - deterministic routing policy derived from the classifier result;
    - A2 selector execution or deterministic-default mode;
    - synchronized effective retrieval-query creation;
    - central SuperPrompt projection for the prepared prompt preview.

    Unimplemented semantic header matching, fuzzy matching, future A2 passes, and downstream retrieval algorithms shall not be presented as current behavior.

    2. 2. Functional Architecture and Responsibility Boundaries
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW
    2.1. Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW The prompt-preparation capability shall accept raw user text and shall produce a SuperPrompt whose current request, meta-configuration, ActiveBrief routing metadata, effective retrieval query, lifecycle state, and prompt preview are ready for downstream context generation.

    The output of this capability shall be the authoritative prepared runtime state for the current request.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY
      2.2. Deterministic Preprocessing Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-BOUNDARY Header parsing, key normalization, canonical-name matching, alias matching, bilingual matching, TASK/CONTEXT fallback, active mandatory-field defaulting, body write-back, and lifecycle bookkeeping shall be deterministic.

      The deterministic preprocessing layer shall not call an LLM and shall not apply fuzzy, semantic, template, cue-based, or probabilistic header inference in the current runtime.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY
      2.3. ActiveBrief Classifier Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-BOUNDARY The ActiveBrief relation classifier shall decide only:
      - prompt_materiality; and
      - topic_relation.

      It shall not decide document-retrieval actions, memory-context actions, final prompt-display actions, pipeline stage order, or downstream evidence behavior.

      Those actions shall be determined by explicit deterministic Python policy.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY
      2.4. A2 PromptShaper Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BOUNDARY A2 PromptShaper shall classify configured prompt meta-fields from the already normalized current request.

      A2 shall not parse raw Markdown headers, select an ActiveBrief, execute Knowledge Retrieval, execute Memory Retrieval, or own final answer generation.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE
      2.5. Agent Stack Infrastructure Reference GHOST-PREPROCESS-AGENT-STACK-REFERENCE ActiveBrief relation classification and A2 PromptShaper shall use the neutral Agent Stack contracts defined in 20_agent_stack.sdoc.

      This file shall own the functional meaning, runtime payload, deterministic validation, routing policy, and SuperPrompt write-back of these concrete prompt functions.

      This file shall not duplicate the generic AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, or LLMClient infrastructure requirements.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY
      2.6. SuperPrompt State Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-SUPERPROMPT-BOUNDARY SuperPrompt shall remain the authoritative shared runtime state for prompt preparation.

      PromptSchema, NameMatcher, ActiveBrief classifier configuration, AgentPrompt, and LLMClient shall not become parallel owners of the current prepared prompt state.

      All persistent prompt-preparation outcomes required by downstream stages shall be written explicitly into SuperPrompt body fields, lifecycle fields, or extras.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY
      2.7. Controller Coordination Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-ORCHESTRATION-BOUNDARY The application controller shall coordinate deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, central prompt projection, and A2 invocation.

      The controller shall not absorb schema normalization rules, classifier semantics, A2 selector sanitization, or downstream retrieval algorithms.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY
      2.8. Knowledge Management Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-BOUNDARY This file shall prepare the effective document-retrieval query and document-retrieval routing state.

      Detailed document ingestion, dense retrieval, SPLADE retrieval, fusion, reranking, usefulness selection, and evidence condensation shall remain owned by the Knowledge Management requirement files.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY
      2.9. Memory Management Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY This file shall prepare the memory-context permission and ActiveBrief continuity state required by downstream memory processing.

      Detailed memory recording, ActiveBrief creation and update, memory ingestion, memory retrieval, memory scoring, and memory-context synthesis shall remain owned by the Memory Management requirement files.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW
      2.1. Raw-Input-to-Retrieval-Handoff Flow GHOST-PREPROCESS-END-TO-END-FLOW The prompt-preparation capability shall accept raw user text and shall produce a SuperPrompt whose current request, meta-configuration, ActiveBrief routing metadata, effective retrieval query, lifecycle state, and prompt preview are ready for downstream context generation.

      The output of this capability shall be the authoritative prepared runtime state for the current request.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT

    The deterministic preprocessing layer shall not call an LLM and shall not apply fuzzy, semantic, template, cue-based, or probabilistic header inference in the current runtime.
