Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY
    2.9. Memory Management Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-BOUNDARY This file shall prepare the memory-context permission and ActiveBrief continuity state required by downstream memory processing.

    Detailed memory recording, ActiveBrief creation and update, memory ingestion, memory retrieval, memory scoring, and memory-context synthesis shall remain owned by the Memory Management requirement files.

    3. 3. Prompt Schema and Canonical Field Contract
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
    3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

    The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON
      3.2. Prompt Schema JSON Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-JSON The current prompt schema shall be represented as a JSON object.

      The executable deterministic preprocessing contract may use:
      - canonical_keys;
      - must_keys;
      - defaults;
      - aliases; and
      - bilingual mappings.

      Additional schema sections shall not be treated as active runtime behavior unless a current requirement and implementation consume them.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION
      3.3. Header-Key Normalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Prompt header keys shall be normalized deterministically by:
      1. accepting string input only;
      2. applying Unicode NFKC normalization;
      3. converting to lowercase;
      4. trimming leading and trailing whitespace;
      5. collapsing internal whitespace runs; and
      6. removing punctuation from the header key used for matching.

      Header-key normalization shall not modify the section body text.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION
        12.6. AC: Header-Key Normalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION Given two header names that differ only by case, Unicode compatibility form, outer whitespace, internal whitespace runs, or punctuation removed by the normalization contract, they shall resolve to the same normalized matching key.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS
      3.4. Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS The current SuperPrompt prompt-body contract shall support these runtime keys:
      - system;
      - task;
      - audience;
      - role;
      - tone;
      - depth;
      - confidence;
      - evidence_policy;
      - context;
      - purpose;
      - format;
      - text.

      Prompt preprocessing shall write only to fields defined by the active SuperPrompt body contract.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING
        3.5. Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-facing canonical names shall have an explicit deterministic mapping to the active SuperPrompt runtime keys.

        In particular:
        - response_depth shall map to depth;
        - output_format shall map to format.

        A schema key shall not be considered successfully applied merely because it was recognized if no runtime destination receives its value.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION
          3.6. Mandatory Field Runtime Destination GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION Every active schema field declared mandatory shall have one defined runtime destination or shall be removed from the active mandatory-field set.

          The system shall not silently claim enforcement of a mandatory schema field whose value is discarded before SuperPrompt write-back.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS
            4.12. Other Mandatory Field Defaults GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS After TASK fallback, each other active mandatory prompt field that remains absent or empty shall receive its configured schema default.

            Defaults shall be applied only when the field has an active runtime destination.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP
          12.11. AC: Schema-to-Runtime Mapping GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP Given a recognized response_depth or output_format value, prompt preprocessing shall route it to runtime depth or format respectively.

          A configured mandatory field without a runtime destination shall fail requirement conformance rather than being reported as enforced.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
      3.7. Canonical Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
        3.8. Alias Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

        The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH
          3.9. Bilingual Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically or through an alias may resolve through a configured bilingual mapping.

          The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER
            3.10. Deterministic Name-Matching Order GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Header resolution shall use this fixed order:
            1. canonical match;
            2. alias match;
            3. bilingual match;
            4. unknown.

            A lower-priority method shall not override a successful higher-priority match.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING
              3.11. No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING The current deterministic header resolver shall not use edit distance, typo correction, templates, keyword cues, embeddings, LLM inference, or semantic similarity.

              Schema data for such future behavior may exist without making that behavior part of the current executable requirement.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER
            12.5. AC: Bilingual Header Resolution GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER Given a heading named Aufgabe and a configured German mapping from aufgabe to task, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime task field.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER
          12.4. AC: Alias Header Resolution GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER Given a heading named Objective and an alias mapping from objective to purpose, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime purpose field.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER
        12.3. AC: Canonical Header Resolution GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER Given a supported heading named TASK, the parser shall resolve it canonically and write its body into the runtime task field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION
      3.12. Prompt Schema Version Traceability GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION The prompt schema shall expose a version identifier sufficient to trace the canonical field vocabulary used by a prompt-preparation baseline.

      An approved change to canonical keys, mandatory fields, defaults, aliases, bilingual mappings, or schema-to-runtime mappings shall update the affected requirements and verification assets.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
      3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

      The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.

    The executable deterministic preprocessing contract may use:
    - canonical_keys;
    - must_keys;
    - defaults;
    - aliases; and
    - bilingual mappings.

    Additional schema sections shall not be treated as active runtime behavior unless a current requirement and implementation consume them.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
    3.7. Canonical Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
      3.8. Alias Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

      The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH
        3.9. Bilingual Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically or through an alias may resolve through a configured bilingual mapping.

        The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER
          3.10. Deterministic Name-Matching Order GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Header resolution shall use this fixed order:
          1. canonical match;
          2. alias match;
          3. bilingual match;
          4. unknown.

          A lower-priority method shall not override a successful higher-priority match.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING
            3.11. No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING The current deterministic header resolver shall not use edit distance, typo correction, templates, keyword cues, embeddings, LLM inference, or semantic similarity.

            Schema data for such future behavior may exist without making that behavior part of the current executable requirement.

    The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER
      3.10. Deterministic Name-Matching Order GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Header resolution shall use this fixed order:
      1. canonical match;
      2. alias match;
      3. bilingual match;
      4. unknown.

      A lower-priority method shall not override a successful higher-priority match.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING
        3.11. No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING The current deterministic header resolver shall not use edit distance, typo correction, templates, keyword cues, embeddings, LLM inference, or semantic similarity.

        Schema data for such future behavior may exist without making that behavior part of the current executable requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER
      12.5. AC: Bilingual Header Resolution GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER Given a heading named Aufgabe and a configured German mapping from aufgabe to task, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime task field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH
      3.9. Bilingual Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically or through an alias may resolve through a configured bilingual mapping.

      The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
        3.8. Alias Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

        The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
          3.7. Canonical Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
            3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

            The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
              1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

              The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER
    3.10. Deterministic Name-Matching Order GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Header resolution shall use this fixed order:
    1. canonical match;
    2. alias match;
    3. bilingual match;
    4. unknown.

    A lower-priority method shall not override a successful higher-priority match.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING
      3.11. No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING The current deterministic header resolver shall not use edit distance, typo correction, templates, keyword cues, embeddings, LLM inference, or semantic similarity.

      Schema data for such future behavior may exist without making that behavior part of the current executable requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER
      3.10. Deterministic Name-Matching Order GHOST-PREPROCESS-MATCH-ORDER Header resolution shall use this fixed order:
      1. canonical match;
      2. alias match;
      3. bilingual match;
      4. unknown.

      A lower-priority method shall not override a successful higher-priority match.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH
        3.9. Bilingual Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically or through an alias may resolve through a configured bilingual mapping.

        The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
          3.8. Alias Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

          The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
            3.7. Canonical Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
              3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

              The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
                1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

                The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

                The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
                • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                  2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING
    3.11. No Current Fuzzy or Semantic Matching GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-FUZZY-MATCHING The current deterministic header resolver shall not use edit distance, typo correction, templates, keyword cues, embeddings, LLM inference, or semantic similarity.

    Schema data for such future behavior may exist without making that behavior part of the current executable requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
      3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

      The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION
    3.12. Prompt Schema Version Traceability GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-VERSION The prompt schema shall expose a version identifier sufficient to trace the canonical field vocabulary used by a prompt-preparation baseline.

    An approved change to canonical keys, mandatory fields, defaults, aliases, bilingual mappings, or schema-to-runtime mappings shall update the affected requirements and verification assets.

    4. 4. Deterministic Prompt Parsing and Name Resolution
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
    4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

    Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
      4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

      Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX
        4.3. Current Header Syntax GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX A current prompt-field heading shall contain:
        - one to three leading hash characters;
        - optional whitespace after the hash characters; and
        - a non-empty header title.

        Therefore both "## TASK" and "##TASK" shall be interpreted according to the same current header-title matching rules.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY
        4.4. Section Body Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY The body of a recognized or unrecognized heading shall extend from the end of that heading to the beginning of the next supported heading or to the end of the raw input.

        Leading and trailing whitespace around each extracted body shall be removed.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
        4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS
          4.7. Repeated Canonical Field Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS When multiple headings resolve to the same canonical field, the body of the last occurrence shall replace the earlier occurrence for the current preprocessing run.

          The parser shall not concatenate repeated field bodies automatically.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS
            12.7. AC: Repeated Field Last Wins GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS Given two supported headings that both resolve to task, the body of the second occurrence shall become the final task value for that preprocessing run.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS
          4.8. Unknown Header Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS A header that cannot be resolved through canonical, alias, or bilingual matching shall not create an arbitrary SuperPrompt body field.

          Its body shall not overwrite a recognized field.

          The current prompt-preparation contract does not require fuzzy recovery of the unknown header.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER
            12.10. AC: Unknown Header Isolation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER Given an unrecognized heading, deterministic preprocessing shall not create a new SuperPrompt body key and shall not overwrite a recognized canonical field.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
          4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

          Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
            4.10. TASK from CONTEXT Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK If structured input contains no non-empty TASK but contains a non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK for the current request.

            The consumed CONTEXT shall then be cleared from the current prompt body so that the same text is not presented twice as both TASK and CONTEXT.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
              12.8. AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK Given structured input with no non-empty TASK and one non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK and the current runtime CONTEXT shall be cleared.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK
            4.11. TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK If structured input contains neither a non-empty TASK nor a non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw user prompt shall become TASK.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK
              12.9. AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK Given structured input with neither non-empty TASK nor non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw input shall become TASK.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION
          4.13. Prompt Body Text Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Deterministic header normalization and matching shall affect header names only.

          Recognized section body text shall preserve the user's wording except for outer whitespace trimming and the explicit TASK/CONTEXT fallback behavior.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE
      4.5. Plain-Text Prompt Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE When no supported heading is present, the complete trimmed raw user text shall become the TASK value.

      Plain-text mode shall not require schema name matching.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK
        12.2. AC: Plain Text Becomes TASK GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK Given raw input with no supported heading, deterministic preprocessing shall write the complete trimmed input into SuperPrompt.body["task"], set stage to preprocessed, append the preprocessing history entry, and preserve retrieval artifacts.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE
      12.1. AC: Whitespace-Only Input GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE Given whitespace-only raw input, the controller shall return the existing SuperPrompt without invoking deterministic preprocessing, relation classification, or A2 and without appending a new preprocessing history entry.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
      4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

      Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
    4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

    Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX
      4.3. Current Header Syntax GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-SYNTAX A current prompt-field heading shall contain:
      - one to three leading hash characters;
      - optional whitespace after the hash characters; and
      - a non-empty header title.

      Therefore both "## TASK" and "##TASK" shall be interpreted according to the same current header-title matching rules.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY
      4.4. Section Body Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-SECTION-BOUNDARY The body of a recognized or unrecognized heading shall extend from the end of that heading to the beginning of the next supported heading or to the end of the raw input.

      Leading and trailing whitespace around each extracted body shall be removed.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
      4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS
        4.7. Repeated Canonical Field Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS When multiple headings resolve to the same canonical field, the body of the last occurrence shall replace the earlier occurrence for the current preprocessing run.

        The parser shall not concatenate repeated field bodies automatically.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS
          12.7. AC: Repeated Field Last Wins GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS Given two supported headings that both resolve to task, the body of the second occurrence shall become the final task value for that preprocessing run.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS
        4.8. Unknown Header Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS A header that cannot be resolved through canonical, alias, or bilingual matching shall not create an arbitrary SuperPrompt body field.

        Its body shall not overwrite a recognized field.

        The current prompt-preparation contract does not require fuzzy recovery of the unknown header.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER
          12.10. AC: Unknown Header Isolation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER Given an unrecognized heading, deterministic preprocessing shall not create a new SuperPrompt body key and shall not overwrite a recognized canonical field.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
        4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

        Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
          4.10. TASK from CONTEXT Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK If structured input contains no non-empty TASK but contains a non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK for the current request.

          The consumed CONTEXT shall then be cleared from the current prompt body so that the same text is not presented twice as both TASK and CONTEXT.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
            12.8. AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK Given structured input with no non-empty TASK and one non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK and the current runtime CONTEXT shall be cleared.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK
          4.11. TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK If structured input contains neither a non-empty TASK nor a non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw user prompt shall become TASK.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK
            12.9. AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK Given structured input with neither non-empty TASK nor non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw input shall become TASK.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION
        4.13. Prompt Body Text Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Deterministic header normalization and matching shall affect header names only.

        Recognized section body text shall preserve the user's wording except for outer whitespace trimming and the explicit TASK/CONTEXT fallback behavior.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
      4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

      The parser shall not concatenate repeated field bodies automatically.

      4.8. Unknown Header Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS A header that cannot be resolved through canonical, alias, or bilingual matching shall not create an arbitrary SuperPrompt body field.

      Its body shall not overwrite a recognized field.

      The current prompt-preparation contract does not require fuzzy recovery of the unknown header.

      4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

      Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.

        4.10. TASK from CONTEXT Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK If structured input contains no non-empty TASK but contains a non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK for the current request.

        The consumed CONTEXT shall then be cleared from the current prompt body so that the same text is not presented twice as both TASK and CONTEXT.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
          12.8. AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK Given structured input with no non-empty TASK and one non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK and the current runtime CONTEXT shall be cleared.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK
        4.11. TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK If structured input contains neither a non-empty TASK nor a non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw user prompt shall become TASK.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK
          12.9. AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK Given structured input with neither non-empty TASK nor non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw input shall become TASK.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION
      4.13. Prompt Body Text Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Deterministic header normalization and matching shall affect header names only.

      Recognized section body text shall preserve the user's wording except for outer whitespace trimming and the explicit TASK/CONTEXT fallback behavior.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
      4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
        4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

        Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
          4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

          Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS
    4.7. Repeated Canonical Field Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS When multiple headings resolve to the same canonical field, the body of the last occurrence shall replace the earlier occurrence for the current preprocessing run.

    The parser shall not concatenate repeated field bodies automatically.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS
      12.7. AC: Repeated Field Last Wins GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS Given two supported headings that both resolve to task, the body of the second occurrence shall become the final task value for that preprocessing run.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
      4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
        4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

        Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
          4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

          Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS
    4.8. Unknown Header Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS A header that cannot be resolved through canonical, alias, or bilingual matching shall not create an arbitrary SuperPrompt body field.

    Its body shall not overwrite a recognized field.

    The current prompt-preparation contract does not require fuzzy recovery of the unknown header.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER
      12.10. AC: Unknown Header Isolation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER Given an unrecognized heading, deterministic preprocessing shall not create a new SuperPrompt body key and shall not overwrite a recognized canonical field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
      4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
        4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

        Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
          4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

          Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
    4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

    Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
      4.10. TASK from CONTEXT Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK If structured input contains no non-empty TASK but contains a non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK for the current request.

      The consumed CONTEXT shall then be cleared from the current prompt body so that the same text is not presented twice as both TASK and CONTEXT.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
        12.8. AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK Given structured input with no non-empty TASK and one non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK and the current runtime CONTEXT shall be cleared.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK
      4.11. TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK If structured input contains neither a non-empty TASK nor a non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw user prompt shall become TASK.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK
        12.9. AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK Given structured input with neither non-empty TASK nor non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw input shall become TASK.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
      4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

      Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
        4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS

          Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
            4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

            Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
              1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

              The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.

    4.10. TASK from CONTEXT Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK If structured input contains no non-empty TASK but contains a non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK for the current request.

    The consumed CONTEXT shall then be cleared from the current prompt body so that the same text is not presented twice as both TASK and CONTEXT.

          Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK
    4.11. TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK If structured input contains neither a non-empty TASK nor a non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw user prompt shall become TASK.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK
      12.9. AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK Given structured input with neither non-empty TASK nor non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw input shall become TASK.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION
      3.6. Mandatory Field Runtime Destination GHOST-PREPROCESS-MUST-DESTINATION Every active schema field declared mandatory shall have one defined runtime destination or shall be removed from the active mandatory-field set.

      The system shall not silently claim enforcement of a mandatory schema field whose value is discarded before SuperPrompt write-back.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING
        3.5. Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-facing canonical names shall have an explicit deterministic mapping to the active SuperPrompt runtime keys.

        In particular:
        - response_depth shall map to depth;
        - output_format shall map to format.

        A schema key shall not be considered successfully applied merely because it was recognized if no runtime destination receives its value.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS
          3.4. Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS The current SuperPrompt prompt-body contract shall support these runtime keys:
          - system;
          - task;
          - audience;
          - role;
          - tone;
          - depth;
          - confidence;
          - evidence_policy;
          - context;
          - purpose;
          - format;
          - text.

          Prompt preprocessing shall write only to fields defined by the active SuperPrompt body contract.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
            3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

            The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
              1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

              The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS
    4.12. Other Mandatory Field Defaults GHOST-PREPROCESS-MANDATORY-DEFAULTS After TASK fallback, each other active mandatory prompt field that remains absent or empty shall receive its configured schema default.

    Defaults shall be applied only when the field has an active runtime destination.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
      4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
        4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

        Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
          4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

          Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION
    4.13. Prompt Body Text Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-TEXT-PRESERVATION Deterministic header normalization and matching shall affect header names only.

    Recognized section body text shall preserve the user's wording except for outer whitespace trimming and the explicit TASK/CONTEXT fallback behavior.

    5. 5. SuperPrompt Construction and Initial Projection
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
    5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

    It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK
      5.2. Canonical Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Recognized and mapped prompt values shall be written into the corresponding active SuperPrompt body fields.

      The write-back shall reflect last-wins behavior, TASK fallback, CONTEXT consumption, and active mandatory-field defaults.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS
        5.3. No Dynamic Body-Field Creation GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS Deterministic preprocessing shall not add new keys outside the active SuperPrompt body contract.

        New canonical prompt fields shall require an approved SuperPrompt contract change and explicit schema-to-runtime mapping.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS
        5.4. Preservation of Untouched Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS A body field not recognized, defaulted, or explicitly cleared by the current preprocessing rules shall retain its existing value.

        Prompt preparation shall not clear retrieval artifacts or unrelated SuperPrompt fields merely because preprocessing is re-run.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION
      5.5. Initial Retrieval-Query Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION After deterministic body write-back, GHOST may build an initial retrieval-oriented text projection from TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT.

      This initial projection shall not be treated as final until ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing have completed.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK
      5.6. Preprocessed Stage Mark GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall set SuperPrompt.stage to "preprocessed".
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK
        5.7. Preprocessing History Entry GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall append "preprocessed" to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION
      5.8. No Retrieval Artifact Mutation GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION Prompt preparation before the retrieval handoff shall not create, rank, remove, or rewrite document chunks, memory candidates, stage ranking views, final document selections, condensed document context, or synthesized memory context.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW
      5.9. Deterministic Intermediate Preview GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW Deterministic preprocessing may create an intermediate prompt preview from the currently populated prompt fields.

      That intermediate preview shall not supersede the final centralized projection performed after relation classification and A2 shaping.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CENTRAL-PROJECTION
        5.10. Central Prompt Projection after Preparation GHOST-PREPROCESS-CENTRAL-PROJECTION At the completion of the composite preprocessing operation, the controller shall invoke the central SuperPrompt projection path so that prompt_ready reflects the current body fields, evidence policy, ActiveBrief routing state, and current lifecycle state.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK
    5.2. Canonical Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Recognized and mapped prompt values shall be written into the corresponding active SuperPrompt body fields.

    The write-back shall reflect last-wins behavior, TASK fallback, CONTEXT consumption, and active mandatory-field defaults.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS
      5.3. No Dynamic Body-Field Creation GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS Deterministic preprocessing shall not add new keys outside the active SuperPrompt body contract.

      New canonical prompt fields shall require an approved SuperPrompt contract change and explicit schema-to-runtime mapping.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS
      5.4. Preservation of Untouched Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS A body field not recognized, defaulted, or explicitly cleared by the current preprocessing rules shall retain its existing value.

      Prompt preparation shall not clear retrieval artifacts or unrelated SuperPrompt fields merely because preprocessing is re-run.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK
      5.2. Canonical Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Recognized and mapped prompt values shall be written into the corresponding active SuperPrompt body fields.

      The write-back shall reflect last-wins behavior, TASK fallback, CONTEXT consumption, and active mandatory-field defaults.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
        5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

        It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS
    5.3. No Dynamic Body-Field Creation GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-NEW-BODY-FIELDS Deterministic preprocessing shall not add new keys outside the active SuperPrompt body contract.

    New canonical prompt fields shall require an approved SuperPrompt contract change and explicit schema-to-runtime mapping.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK
      5.2. Canonical Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-BODY-WRITEBACK Recognized and mapped prompt values shall be written into the corresponding active SuperPrompt body fields.

      The write-back shall reflect last-wins behavior, TASK fallback, CONTEXT consumption, and active mandatory-field defaults.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
        5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

        It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS
    5.4. Preservation of Untouched Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-PRESERVE-UNTOUCHED-FIELDS A body field not recognized, defaulted, or explicitly cleared by the current preprocessing rules shall retain its existing value.

    Prompt preparation shall not clear retrieval artifacts or unrelated SuperPrompt fields merely because preprocessing is re-run.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION
    5.5. Initial Retrieval-Query Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-INITIAL-QUERY-PROJECTION After deterministic body write-back, GHOST may build an initial retrieval-oriented text projection from TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT.

    This initial projection shall not be treated as final until ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing have completed.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK
    5.6. Preprocessed Stage Mark GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall set SuperPrompt.stage to "preprocessed".
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK
      5.7. Preprocessing History Entry GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall append "preprocessed" to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK
      5.6. Preprocessed Stage Mark GHOST-PREPROCESS-STAGE-MARK Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall set SuperPrompt.stage to "preprocessed".
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
        5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

        It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK
    5.7. Preprocessing History Entry GHOST-PREPROCESS-HISTORY-MARK Successful deterministic preprocessing of non-empty input shall append "preprocessed" to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION
    5.8. No Retrieval Artifact Mutation GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION Prompt preparation before the retrieval handoff shall not create, rank, remove, or rewrite document chunks, memory candidates, stage ranking views, final document selections, condensed document context, or synthesized memory context.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
      5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

      It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW
    5.9. Deterministic Intermediate Preview GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW Deterministic preprocessing may create an intermediate prompt preview from the currently populated prompt fields.

    That intermediate preview shall not supersede the final centralized projection performed after relation classification and A2 shaping.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CENTRAL-PROJECTION
      5.10. Central Prompt Projection after Preparation GHOST-PREPROCESS-CENTRAL-PROJECTION At the completion of the composite preprocessing operation, the controller shall invoke the central SuperPrompt projection path so that prompt_ready reflects the current body fields, evidence policy, ActiveBrief routing state, and current lifecycle state.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW
      5.9. Deterministic Intermediate Preview GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOCAL-PREVIEW Deterministic preprocessing may create an intermediate prompt preview from the currently populated prompt fields.

      That intermediate preview shall not supersede the final centralized projection performed after relation classification and A2 shaping.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE
        5.1. In-Place SuperPrompt Update GHOST-PREPROCESS-IN-PLACE-UPDATE Deterministic preprocessing shall update the SuperPrompt instance supplied by the orchestrator.

        It shall not create a hidden parallel prompt object or replace the active SuperPrompt without an explicit orchestration requirement.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
