        12.23. AC: A2 Deterministic-Default Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT Given use_llm = false and valid configured defaults, A2 shall produce a sanitized selector result without an LLM call, mark a2_llm_used false, and mark its mode as deterministic_defaults.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS
      8.7. A2 Configured Model Settings GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS A2 shall use model name, temperature where applicable, and maximum output-token allowance obtained from its AgentPrompt configuration.

      A2 business logic shall not silently replace configured model settings with unrelated hard-coded values.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
      8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

      The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
        8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

        Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS
    8.3. A2 Decision Targets GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-TARGETS The current A2 PromptShaper shall classify these six fields:
    - system;
    - audience;
    - tone;
    - depth;
    - confidence;
    - evidence_policy.

    System may select one to three configured role identifiers. Each other field shall select exactly one configured identifier.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
      8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

      Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD
    8.4. A2 Runtime Payload GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PAYLOAD A2 shall build its prompt-under-evaluation from the available PURPOSE, TASK, and CONTEXT values of the current SuperPrompt.

    A2 shall also provide an explicit required-output JSON shape derived from the active configured decision targets.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
      8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

      The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
        8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

        Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
    8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
    1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
    2. compose the selector messages;
    3. call the configured LLM path;
    4. parse the structured result; and
    5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
      8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

      The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL
        8.9. A2 Invalid Identifier Removal GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL For each A2 field, deterministic sanitization shall remove option identifiers that are not present in that field's allowed catalog.

        This rule shall remove invented identifiers, malformed identifiers, and identifiers copied from another field's catalog.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID
          12.21. AC: A2 Invalid Identifier Sanitization GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID Given an A2 result containing an invented identifier or an identifier from another field's catalog, the sanitizer shall remove that identifier before SuperPrompt write-back.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL
        8.10. A2 Duplicate Identifier Removal GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DUPLICATE-REMOVAL Deterministic A2 sanitization shall remove duplicate option identifiers while preserving the first valid occurrence order.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY
        8.11. A2 Cardinality Enforcement GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY A2 sanitization shall enforce each active field's configured maximum selection count.

        When more valid identifiers are returned than allowed, the first valid identifiers up to the configured maximum shall be retained.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE
        8.12. Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE If an A2 field has no valid identifier after sanitization, A2 shall omit that field from write-back.

        A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value and shall not apply a catalog default as semantic repair after invalid LLM output.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE
          12.22. AC: A2 Empty Sanitized Field Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE Given an A2 field whose candidate output becomes empty after sanitization, A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value by omitting that field from write-back.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
        8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

        When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

        For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS
          8.14. A2 Diagnostic Metadata GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS A2 shall write stable runtime metadata sufficient to identify:
          - the sanitized selected identifiers;
          - whether the LLM was used; and
          - whether execution used LLM mode or deterministic-default mode.

          Current keys shall include a2_selected_ids, a2_llm_used, and a2_mode.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
          8.15. A2 Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

          A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE
            8.16. A2 Stage Mark GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE After successful A2 processing, SuperPrompt.stage shall be set to "a2".
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY
              8.17. A2 History Entry GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY After successful A2 processing, "a2" shall be appended to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE
              12.25. AC: A2 Lifecycle and Artifact Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE After successful A2 execution, stage shall equal a2, history shall contain the new a2 entry, prompt_ready shall reflect the A2 selections, and existing document or memory retrieval artifacts shall remain unchanged.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION
            8.18. A2 Prompt Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION After A2 write-back, GHOST shall rebuild prompt_ready through the current central SuperPrompt composition contract so that selected meta-field labels and evidence policy are visible in the prepared prompt preview.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION
            8.19. A2 Retrieval-State Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 shall not create, remove, reorder, or rewrite document retrieval results, memory retrieval results, evidence selections, condensed context, or retrieval-stage score views.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN
            8.20. Safe A2 Re-Execution GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN The orchestrator may re-run A2 after preprocessing when meta-field refinement is required.

            Re-running A2 shall apply the same sanitization and write-back rules and shall not clear existing retrieval artifacts solely because the lifecycle stage returns to "a2".
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL
          12.24. AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL Given valid sanitized A2 identifiers with configured labels, SuperPrompt extras shall retain the identifiers while the body fields contain their human-readable labels.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
      8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

      The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
        8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

        Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE
    8.6. A2 Deterministic-Default Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE When A2 LLM mode is disabled, A2 shall construct its candidate selector result deterministically from configured defaults.

    If a configured default is absent, A2 may use the first allowed configured option or the allowed prefix required by a multi-selection target.

    The same deterministic sanitization and write-back rules shall apply after candidate construction.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT
      12.23. AC: A2 Deterministic-Default Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT Given use_llm = false and valid configured defaults, A2 shall produce a sanitized selector result without an LLM call, mark a2_llm_used false, and mark its mode as deterministic_defaults.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
      8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

      The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
        8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

        Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS
    8.7. A2 Configured Model Settings GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-MODEL-SETTINGS A2 shall use model name, temperature where applicable, and maximum output-token allowance obtained from its AgentPrompt configuration.

    A2 business logic shall not silently replace configured model settings with unrelated hard-coded values.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
      8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
      1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
      2. compose the selector messages;
      3. call the configured LLM path;
      4. parse the structured result; and
      5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG

        The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.

    The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.

      This rule shall remove invented identifiers, malformed identifiers, and identifiers copied from another field's catalog.

      A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value and shall not apply a catalog default as semantic repair after invalid LLM output.

        12.22. AC: A2 Empty Sanitized Field Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE Given an A2 field whose candidate output becomes empty after sanitization, A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value by omitting that field from write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
      8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

      When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

      For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.

        8.14. A2 Diagnostic Metadata GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS A2 shall write stable runtime metadata sufficient to identify:
        - the sanitized selected identifiers;

        - whether execution used LLM mode or deterministic-default mode.

        Current keys shall include a2_selected_ids, a2_llm_used, and a2_mode.

        8.15. A2 Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

        A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.

          8.16. A2 Stage Mark GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE After successful A2 processing, SuperPrompt.stage shall be set to "a2".
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY
            8.17. A2 History Entry GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY After successful A2 processing, "a2" shall be appended to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.

          8.18. A2 Prompt Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION After A2 write-back, GHOST shall rebuild prompt_ready through the current central SuperPrompt composition contract so that selected meta-field labels and evidence policy are visible in the prepared prompt preview.

          8.19. A2 Retrieval-State Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 shall not create, remove, reorder, or rewrite document retrieval results, memory retrieval results, evidence selections, condensed context, or retrieval-stage score views.

          8.20. Safe A2 Re-Execution GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN The orchestrator may re-run A2 after preprocessing when meta-field refinement is required.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

              The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY
    8.11. A2 Cardinality Enforcement GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CARDINALITY A2 sanitization shall enforce each active field's configured maximum selection count.

    When more valid identifiers are returned than allowed, the first valid identifiers up to the configured maximum shall be retained.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
      8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

      The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
        8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
        1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
        2. compose the selector messages;
        3. call the configured LLM path;
        4. parse the structured result; and
        5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
          8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

          The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
            8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

            Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
              1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

              The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE
    8.12. Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE If an A2 field has no valid identifier after sanitization, A2 shall omit that field from write-back.

    A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value and shall not apply a catalog default as semantic repair after invalid LLM output.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE
      12.22. AC: A2 Empty Sanitized Field Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE Given an A2 field whose candidate output becomes empty after sanitization, A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value by omitting that field from write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
      8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

      The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
        8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
        1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
        2. compose the selector messages;
        3. call the configured LLM path;
        4. parse the structured result; and
        5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
          8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

          The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
            8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

            Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
              1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

              The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
    8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

    When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

    For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS
      8.14. A2 Diagnostic Metadata GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EXTRAS A2 shall write stable runtime metadata sufficient to identify:
      - the sanitized selected identifiers;
      - whether the LLM was used; and
      - whether execution used LLM mode or deterministic-default mode.

      Current keys shall include a2_selected_ids, a2_llm_used, and a2_mode.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
      8.15. A2 Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

      A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE
        8.16. A2 Stage Mark GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE After successful A2 processing, SuperPrompt.stage shall be set to "a2".
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY
          8.17. A2 History Entry GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-HISTORY After successful A2 processing, "a2" shall be appended to SuperPrompt.history_of_stages.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE
          12.25. AC: A2 Lifecycle and Artifact Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE After successful A2 execution, stage shall equal a2, history shall contain the new a2 entry, prompt_ready shall reflect the A2 selections, and existing document or memory retrieval artifacts shall remain unchanged.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION
        8.18. A2 Prompt Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-PROJECTION After A2 write-back, GHOST shall rebuild prompt_ready through the current central SuperPrompt composition contract so that selected meta-field labels and evidence policy are visible in the prepared prompt preview.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION
        8.19. A2 Retrieval-State Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 shall not create, remove, reorder, or rewrite document retrieval results, memory retrieval results, evidence selections, condensed context, or retrieval-stage score views.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN
        8.20. Safe A2 Re-Execution GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN The orchestrator may re-run A2 after preprocessing when meta-field refinement is required.

        Re-running A2 shall apply the same sanitization and write-back rules and shall not clear existing retrieval artifacts solely because the lifecycle stage returns to "a2".
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL
      12.24. AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL Given valid sanitized A2 identifiers with configured labels, SuperPrompt extras shall retain the identifiers while the body fields contain their human-readable labels.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
      8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

      When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

      For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
        8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

        The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.

      A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
        8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

        When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

        For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
          8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

          The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
            8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
            1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
            2. compose the selector messages;
            3. call the configured LLM path;
            4. parse the structured result; and
            5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
              8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

              The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
                8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

                Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
                • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
                  1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

                  The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

                  The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
                  • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                    2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION
    8.19. A2 Retrieval-State Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-NO-RETRIEVAL-MUTATION A2 shall not create, remove, reorder, or rewrite document retrieval results, memory retrieval results, evidence selections, condensed context, or retrieval-stage score views.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
      8.15. A2 Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

      A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
        8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

        When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

        For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
          8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

          The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
            8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
            1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
            2. compose the selector messages;
            3. call the configured LLM path;
            4. parse the structured result; and
            5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
              8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

              The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
                8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

                Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
                • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
                  1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

                  The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

                  The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
                  • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                    2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN
    8.20. Safe A2 Re-Execution GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-RERUN The orchestrator may re-run A2 after preprocessing when meta-field refinement is required.

    Re-running A2 shall apply the same sanitization and write-back rules and shall not clear existing retrieval artifacts solely because the lifecycle stage returns to "a2".

    9. 9. Prepared Handoff to Knowledge and Memory Retrieval
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
    9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

    The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE
      9.2. Retrieval Query Source Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE The current prompt portion of the effective retrieval query shall be derived from available TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT values.

      A2 meta-fields such as system, audience, tone, depth, confidence, and evidence_policy shall not be inserted into the document-retrieval query merely because they appear in the final prompt preview.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF
      9.3. ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief content shall contribute to the effective retrieval query only according to the deterministic routing state:
      - WEAK + SAME_TOPIC: full ActiveBrief body;
      - WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN: ActiveBrief title;
      - all STRONG states: no ActiveBrief contribution;
      - WEAK + IRRELEVANT: empty query;
      - NO_ACTIVEBRIEF: current prompt only.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF
      9.4. Knowledge Retrieval Handoff GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF The Knowledge Management path shall receive:
      - the prepared SuperPrompt;
      - the synchronized effective retrieval-query text; and
      - routing state sufficient to determine whether document retrieval is applicable.

      This handoff shall not precompute document candidates.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION
        9.8. No Knowledge Retrieval Execution GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION The prompt-preparation capability shall not query ChromaDB, SPLADE indexes, document manifests, rerankers, A3, or A4.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL
          12.27. AC: No Retrieval inside Prompt Preparation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL Executing the complete prompt-preparation capability shall not query document or memory vector stores and shall not run reranking, A3, A4, or memory synthesis.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF
      9.5. Memory Retrieval Handoff GHOST-PREPROCESS-MEMORY-HANDOFF The Memory Management path shall receive:
      - the prepared SuperPrompt;
      - the ActiveBrief snapshot and relation state;
      - allow_memory_context; and
      - memory_context_policy.

      This handoff shall not precompute or synthesize memory context.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION
        9.9. No Memory Retrieval Execution GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-MEMORY-EXECUTION The prompt-preparation capability shall not query memory vector stores, run memory candidate scoring, merge memory records, or synthesize final memory context.

        Reading the current eligible ActiveBrief for relation classification shall not be treated as full Memory Retrieval execution.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL
      9.6. Empty Retrieval Query as Suppression Signal GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-QUERY-SIGNAL An empty effective document-retrieval query produced by WEAK + IRRELEVANT routing shall be treated as an explicit document-retrieval suppression signal.

      Downstream orchestration shall skip document retrieval or handle the state without attempting a normal retrieval call with an empty query.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY
      9.7. Prepared-State Consistency GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY At handoff, the following shall describe the same current request and routing decision:
      - SuperPrompt body fields;
      - ActiveBrief relation extras;
      - deterministic routing decision;
      - effective retrieval-query text;
      - A2 selected identifiers and display labels;
      - lifecycle stage and history;
      - prompt_ready.

      Contradictory representations shall be treated as a defect.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY
        9.10. Current Request Priority in Prepared Prompt GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY The prepared prompt preview shall present the current user request as the highest-priority task.

        Any ActiveBrief continuity material shall be presented only as supporting context according to final_prompt_mode.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF
        12.26. AC: Prepared Retrieval Handoff GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF At completion of prompt preparation, the SuperPrompt body, routing extras, effective retrieval query, A2 metadata, stage/history, and prompt_ready shall be mutually consistent and sufficient for Knowledge and Memory Retrieval to begin without reparsing the raw user prompt.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD
      12.19. AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD Given an initial pre-classification query and a final routing state that changes ActiveBrief contribution or suppresses retrieval, the effective retrieval-query field shall be rebuilt after classification and shall reflect the final state at handoff.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE
    9.2. Retrieval Query Source Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-SOURCE The current prompt portion of the effective retrieval query shall be derived from available TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT values.

    A2 meta-fields such as system, audience, tone, depth, confidence, and evidence_policy shall not be inserted into the document-retrieval query merely because they appear in the final prompt preview.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.

    9.3. ActiveBrief Contribution to Retrieval Query GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief content shall contribute to the effective retrieval query only according to the deterministic routing state:
    - WEAK + SAME_TOPIC: full ActiveBrief body;
    - WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN: ActiveBrief title;
    - all STRONG states: no ActiveBrief contribution;
    - WEAK + IRRELEVANT: empty query;
    - NO_ACTIVEBRIEF: current prompt only.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF
    9.4. Knowledge Retrieval Handoff GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF The Knowledge Management path shall receive:
    - the prepared SuperPrompt;
    - the synchronized effective retrieval-query text; and
    - routing state sufficient to determine whether document retrieval is applicable.

    This handoff shall not precompute document candidates.

        12.27. AC: No Retrieval inside Prompt Preparation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL Executing the complete prompt-preparation capability shall not query document or memory vector stores and shall not run reranking, A3, A4, or memory synthesis.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY
    9.7. Prepared-State Consistency GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY At handoff, the following shall describe the same current request and routing decision:
    - SuperPrompt body fields;
    - ActiveBrief relation extras;
    - deterministic routing decision;
    - effective retrieval-query text;
    - A2 selected identifiers and display labels;
    - lifecycle stage and history;
    - prompt_ready.

    Contradictory representations shall be treated as a defect.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY
      9.10. Current Request Priority in Prepared Prompt GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY The prepared prompt preview shall present the current user request as the highest-priority task.

      Any ActiveBrief continuity material shall be presented only as supporting context according to final_prompt_mode.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF
      12.26. AC: Prepared Retrieval Handoff GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF At completion of prompt preparation, the SuperPrompt body, routing extras, effective retrieval query, A2 metadata, stage/history, and prompt_ready shall be mutually consistent and sufficient for Knowledge and Memory Retrieval to begin without reparsing the raw user prompt.

      9.7. Prepared-State Consistency GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY At handoff, the following shall describe the same current request and routing decision:
      - SuperPrompt body fields;
      - ActiveBrief relation extras;
      - deterministic routing decision;
      - effective retrieval-query text;
      - A2 selected identifiers and display labels;
      - lifecycle stage and history;
      - prompt_ready.

      Contradictory representations shall be treated as a defect.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
        9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

        The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY
    9.10. Current Request Priority in Prepared Prompt GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-REQUEST-PRIORITY The prepared prompt preview shall present the current user request as the highest-priority task.

    Any ActiveBrief continuity material shall be presented only as supporting context according to final_prompt_mode.

    10. 10. Error Handling, Logging, and Quality Requirements
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.

        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR
    10.1. Prompt Schema Failure GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-ERROR A missing, unreadable, malformed, or non-object prompt schema shall fail explicitly during schema initialization.

    GHOST shall not silently continue with an invented schema when deterministic preprocessing depends on that configuration.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR
    10.2. Relation Classifier Failure Integrity GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-ERROR A provider failure, parse failure, missing required binding, or invalid classifier value shall not produce a fabricated STRONG, WEAK, SAME_TOPIC, RELATED_DOMAIN, or IRRELEVANT result.

    Fallback behavior, if introduced later, shall require an explicit approved requirement.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR
    10.3. A2 Failure Integrity GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ERROR A2 provider or parse failure shall not be represented as a successful LLM selection.

    The deterministic-default mode shall be used only when explicitly selected by the caller, not as an undisclosed replacement for a failed LLM call.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL
    10.4. No Unnecessary Relation-Classifier Call GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-UNNECESSARY-CLASSIFIER-CALL GHOST shall not invoke the relation-classifier LLM when no eligible ActiveBrief exists or when the classifier current-prompt text is empty.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY
    10.5. Prompt Preparation Logging Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-LOGGING-BOUNDARY Prompt preparation shall emit compact public lifecycle and relation summaries and may emit richer internal or developer diagnostics according to the logging policy.

    Raw prompt payloads, ActiveBrief bodies, model messages, and raw model outputs shall not be exposed through public logs by default.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY
    10.6. Deterministic Reproducibility GHOST-PREPROCESS-DETERMINISTIC-REPRODUCIBILITY Given the same raw prompt, prompt schema, starting relevant SuperPrompt body state, and no LLM-enabled step, deterministic preprocessing shall produce the same recognized field values and routing-independent intermediate query text.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY
    10.7. Prompt Function Testability GHOST-PREPROCESS-TESTABILITY Prompt parsing, name resolution, schema-to-runtime mapping, TASK fallback, routing-table construction, query projection, A2 sanitization, and SuperPrompt write-back shall be testable independently of the GUI.

    LLM-enabled classifier and A2 tests shall support provider stubs or recorded structured responses so deterministic post-processing can be verified.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS
    10.8. No Hidden Prompt Heuristics GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-HIDDEN-HEURISTICS Current prompt-preparation behavior shall not depend on undocumented fuzzy matching, semantic thresholds, hidden prompt repairs, or implicit ActiveBrief inclusion rules.

    Every current deterministic action shall be traceable to an explicit requirement and testable rule.

    11. 11. Traceability and Sibling Requirement Ownership
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS
    11.1. Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt-preparation requirements shall remain traceable to the modules and configurations that implement schema loading, deterministic parsing, name matching, ActiveBrief relation classification, A2 shaping, SuperPrompt mutation, query projection, and controller coordination.

    A private helper shall not become the owner of a system requirement merely because it currently contains the implementing function.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION
      11.3. Requirement and Implementation Synchronization GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION An approved change to prompt schema semantics, deterministic parsing, canonical field mapping, ActiveBrief routing, A2 targets, query projection, public interfaces, or SuperPrompt write-back shall update the affected requirements and verification assets together with the implementation change.

      Private refactoring that preserves required behavior and interfaces shall not require new functional requirements.

      Code that contradicts an approved requirement in this file shall be treated as a defect or unapproved change until the contradiction is resolved.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION
        12.28. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION An implementation baseline shall not be accepted when its prompt-schema mapping, routing table, query projection, final prompt projection, or A2 sanitization contradicts an approved requirement in this file.

        The contradiction shall be resolved by correcting the defect or approving a coordinated requirement and implementation change.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
      1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

      The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

      The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP
    11.2. Sibling Requirement Ownership GHOST-PREPROCESS-SIBLING-OWNERSHIP Detailed behavior shall remain in the following sibling requirement areas:

    - neutral AgentFactory, AgentPrompt, and LLMClient infrastructure in 20_agent_stack.sdoc;
    - runtime stage coordination in 10_orchestrator_and_pipeline.sdoc;
    - document ingestion in 41_document_ingestion.sdoc;
    - document retrieval and reranking in 42_document_retrieval_and_reranking.sdoc;
    - document usefulness selection and condensation in 43_evidence_selection_and_condensation.sdoc;
    - memory recording and ActiveBrief creation/update in 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc;
    - memory ingestion and retrieval in 52_memory_ingestion_and_retrieval.sdoc;
    - memory merge, ActiveBrief continuity, and Blackboard direction in 53_memory_merge_activebrief_blackboard.sdoc;
    - current Streamlit controls and preview rendering in 61_streamlit_workbench_current.sdoc;
    - logging in 81_logging_textforge_raglog.sdoc;
    - security and traceability governance in 83_quality_security_traceability_governance.sdoc.

    This file shall not duplicate those subsystem algorithms.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS
      11.1. Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt-preparation requirements shall remain traceable to the modules and configurations that implement schema loading, deterministic parsing, name matching, ActiveBrief relation classification, A2 shaping, SuperPrompt mutation, query projection, and controller coordination.

      A private helper shall not become the owner of a system requirement merely because it currently contains the implementing function.

        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY
    11.4. Current and Future Prompt-Function Boundary GHOST-PREPROCESS-CURRENT-FUTURE-BOUNDARY The current requirement baseline shall not present the following as implemented unless corresponding code and tests exist:
    - fuzzy or edit-distance header matching;
    - semantic or LLM-based header recovery;
    - template or keyword-cue field inference;
    - automatic multi-pass A2 refinement;
    - new prompt body fields without SuperPrompt support;
    - new relation labels or routing states;
    - retrieval or memory algorithms inside preprocessing.

    Such capabilities shall be introduced through explicit future requirements and controlled implementation changes.

    12. 12. Acceptance Criteria
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
      4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

      Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE
    12.1. AC: Whitespace-Only Input GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WHITESPACE Given whitespace-only raw input, the controller shall return the existing SuperPrompt without invoking deterministic preprocessing, relation classification, or A2 and without appending a new preprocessing history entry.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE
      4.5. Plain-Text Prompt Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-PLAIN-TEXT-MODE When no supported heading is present, the complete trimmed raw user text shall become the TASK value.

      Plain-text mode shall not require schema name matching.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
        4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

        Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK
    12.2. AC: Plain Text Becomes TASK GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-PLAIN-TASK Given raw input with no supported heading, deterministic preprocessing shall write the complete trimmed input into SuperPrompt.body["task"], set stage to preprocessed, append the preprocessing history entry, and preserve retrieval artifacts.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
      3.7. Canonical Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
        3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

        The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.

            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER
    12.3. AC: Canonical Header Resolution GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CANONICAL-HEADER Given a supported heading named TASK, the parser shall resolve it canonically and write its body into the runtime task field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
      3.8. Alias Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

      The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
        3.7. Canonical Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
          3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

          The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.

            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER
    12.4. AC: Alias Header Resolution GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-ALIAS-HEADER Given a heading named Objective and an alias mapping from objective to purpose, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime purpose field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH
      3.9. Bilingual Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-BILINGUAL-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically or through an alias may resolve through a configured bilingual mapping.

      The current schema may provide German-to-canonical mappings without requiring language detection.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH
        3.8. Alias Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-ALIAS-MATCH A normalized header not matched canonically may resolve through the configured alias map.

        The alias target shall resolve to an active canonical key before runtime write-back.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH
          3.7. Canonical Header Match GHOST-PREPROCESS-CANONICAL-MATCH A normalized header matching an active canonical schema key shall resolve directly to that canonical key.

            3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

            The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.

              1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.
