    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER
    12.5. AC: Bilingual Header Resolution GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-BILINGUAL-HEADER Given a heading named Aufgabe and a configured German mapping from aufgabe to task, the parser shall write the heading body into the runtime task field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION
      3.3. Header-Key Normalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-NORMALIZATION Prompt header keys shall be normalized deterministically by:
      1. accepting string input only;
      2. applying Unicode NFKC normalization;
      3. converting to lowercase;
      4. trimming leading and trailing whitespace;
      5. collapsing internal whitespace runs; and
      6. removing punctuation from the header key used for matching.

      Header-key normalization shall not modify the section body text.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
        3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

        The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION
    12.6. AC: Header-Key Normalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NORMALIZATION Given two header names that differ only by case, Unicode compatibility form, outer whitespace, internal whitespace runs, or punctuation removed by the normalization contract, they shall resolve to the same normalized matching key.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS
      4.7. Repeated Canonical Field Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-LAST-WINS When multiple headings resolve to the same canonical field, the body of the last occurrence shall replace the earlier occurrence for the current preprocessing run.

      The parser shall not concatenate repeated field bodies automatically.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
        4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
          4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

          Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
            4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

            Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
              1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

              The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS
    12.7. AC: Repeated Field Last Wins GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-LAST-WINS Given two supported headings that both resolve to task, the body of the second occurrence shall become the final task value for that preprocessing run.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
      4.10. TASK from CONTEXT Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-CONTEXT-FALLBACK If structured input contains no non-empty TASK but contains a non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK for the current request.

      The consumed CONTEXT shall then be cleared from the current prompt body so that the same text is not presented twice as both TASK and CONTEXT.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
        4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

        Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
          4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
            4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

            Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
              4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

              Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
                1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

                The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

                The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
                • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                  2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK
    12.8. AC: CONTEXT Promoted to TASK GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CONTEXT-FALLBACK Given structured input with no non-empty TASK and one non-empty CONTEXT, the CONTEXT body shall become TASK and the current runtime CONTEXT shall be cleared.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK
      4.11. TASK from Raw Prompt Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-TASK-RAW-FALLBACK If structured input contains neither a non-empty TASK nor a non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw user prompt shall become TASK.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK
        4.9. Recognized Field Write-Back Only GHOST-PREPROCESS-RECOGNIZED-WRITEBACK Only values resolved to active runtime prompt fields through the schema-to-runtime mapping shall be eligible for SuperPrompt body write-back.

        Schema metadata, unknown headings, and unsupported canonical fields shall not create new dynamic body keys.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
          4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
            4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

            Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
              4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

              Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
                1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

                The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

                The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
                • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                  2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK
    12.9. AC: Raw Prompt TASK Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-RAW-FALLBACK Given structured input with neither non-empty TASK nor non-empty CONTEXT, the complete trimmed raw input shall become TASK.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS
      4.8. Unknown Header Handling GHOST-PREPROCESS-UNKNOWN-HEADERS A header that cannot be resolved through canonical, alias, or bilingual matching shall not create an arbitrary SuperPrompt body field.

      Its body shall not overwrite a recognized field.

      The current prompt-preparation contract does not require fuzzy recovery of the unknown header.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION
        4.6. Header Resolution through NameMatcher GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-RESOLUTION For structured input, each extracted header title shall be passed through the deterministic name-matching contract before its body can be written into a canonical prompt field.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS
          4.2. Supported Header Levels GHOST-PREPROCESS-HEADER-LEVELS Structured prompt parsing shall recognize headings beginning with one, two, or three hash characters.

          Headings deeper than three levels shall not be treated as prompt-field headers by the current parser.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT
            4.1. Non-Empty Raw Input Processing GHOST-PREPROCESS-NONEMPTY-INPUT The controller shall trim the raw user text before starting prompt preparation.

            Whitespace-only input shall return the existing SuperPrompt without adding a preprocessing stage entry or invoking the ActiveBrief classifier.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
              1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

              The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER
    12.10. AC: Unknown Header Isolation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-UNKNOWN-HEADER Given an unrecognized heading, deterministic preprocessing shall not create a new SuperPrompt body key and shall not overwrite a recognized canonical field.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING
      3.5. Schema-to-Runtime Field Mapping GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAPPING Schema-facing canonical names shall have an explicit deterministic mapping to the active SuperPrompt runtime keys.

      In particular:
      - response_depth shall map to depth;
      - output_format shall map to format.

      A schema key shall not be considered successfully applied merely because it was recognized if no runtime destination receives its value.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS
        3.4. Current Runtime Prompt Body Fields GHOST-PREPROCESS-RUNTIME-BODY-FIELDS The current SuperPrompt prompt-body contract shall support these runtime keys:
        - system;
        - task;
        - audience;
        - role;
        - tone;
        - depth;
        - confidence;
        - evidence_policy;
        - context;
        - purpose;
        - format;
        - text.

        Prompt preprocessing shall write only to fields defined by the active SuperPrompt body contract.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD
          3.1. Prompt Schema Loading GHOST-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA-LOAD The controller shall load the prompt schema from a configured JSON path and shall make one reusable PromptSchema instance available to deterministic preprocessing.

          The schema shall be loaded before prompt parsing begins.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP
    12.11. AC: Schema-to-Runtime Mapping GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SCHEMA-RUNTIME-MAP Given a recognized response_depth or output_format value, prompt preprocessing shall route it to runtime depth or format respectively.

    A configured mandatory field without a runtime destination shall fail requirement conformance rather than being reported as enforced.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF
      7.10. Routing State: No ActiveBrief GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF For NO_ACTIVEBRIEF, GHOST shall set:
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = false;
      - final_prompt_mode = none;
      - allow_memory_context = true;
      - memory_context_policy = normal_no_activebrief.

      The effective document query shall use the current prompt only.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT
        7.9. Deterministic Routing Decision Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT GHOST shall deterministically derive one routing decision containing:
        - prompt_materiality;
        - topic_relation;
        - use_activebrief_for_retrieval;
        - final_prompt_mode;
        - allow_memory_context; and
        - memory_context_policy.

        The LLM shall not populate these action fields directly.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION
          7.1. Two Independent Classification Dimensions GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION The relation classifier shall return exactly two semantic decisions:
          - prompt_materiality; and
          - topic_relation.

          The classifier shall not collapse both dimensions into one combined LLM label.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF
    12.12. AC: No ActiveBrief State GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-ACTIVEBRIEF Given no eligible ActiveBrief, relation classification shall not call the LLM and shall write UNKNOWN, NO_ACTIVEBRIEF, an empty ActiveBrief snapshot, and the normal_no_activebrief routing decision.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-CURRENT-BYPASS
      6.7. Empty Current-Prompt Bypass GHOST-PREPROCESS-EMPTY-CURRENT-BYPASS If an eligible ActiveBrief exists but the classifier current-prompt text remains empty, GHOST shall not invoke the LLM.

      It shall deterministically record:
      - prompt_materiality = WEAK;
      - topic_relation = IRRELEVANT;
      - relation classifier LLM used = false.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-RAW-FALLBACK
        6.6. Classifier Raw-Text Fallback GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-RAW-FALLBACK If the normalized PURPOSE, TASK, and CONTEXT projection is empty, the classifier input builder may use the trimmed raw user text as the current prompt.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-CURRENT-PROMPT
          6.5. Classifier Current-Prompt Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-CURRENT-PROMPT When an eligible ActiveBrief exists, the relation classifier shall receive the normalized current prompt built from available PURPOSE, TASK, and CONTEXT values.

          The current prompt shall remain distinguishable from the ActiveBrief title and body.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-ELIGIBILITY
            6.2. Current Eligible ActiveBrief GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-ELIGIBILITY When a memory manager is available, the classifier input shall use the latest eligible memory record, searched from newest to oldest, that:
            - is not tagged Black; and
            - contains a non-empty ActiveRetrievalBrief body.

            The semantic meaning and lifecycle of memory tags and ActiveRetrievalBrief persistence shall remain owned by the Memory Management requirements.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL
              6.1. Optional ActiveBrief Input GHOST-PREPROCESS-ACTIVEBRIEF-OPTIONAL Prompt preparation shall support execution with or without a configured memory manager and with or without an eligible ActiveBrief.

              Absence of an ActiveBrief shall be a valid routing state and shall not be treated as an LLM failure.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
                1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

                The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

                The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
                • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                  2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-EMPTY-CURRENT
    12.13. AC: Empty Current Prompt with ActiveBrief GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-EMPTY-CURRENT Given an eligible ActiveBrief but no usable current-prompt text, relation classification shall not call the LLM and shall write WEAK + IRRELEVANT with memory context disabled and final_prompt_mode none.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-VALIDATION
      7.7. Classifier Result Validation GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-VALIDATION After AgentPrompt parsing, GHOST shall validate that prompt_materiality and topic_relation belong to their exact allowed value sets.

      An invalid or unknown value shall fail explicitly and shall not be converted into a fabricated valid routing state.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
        7.6. Exact Classifier Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT The relation-classifier LLM shall return one JSON object containing exactly:
        - prompt_materiality; and
        - topic_relation.

        It shall not return action decisions, explanations, prose, or additional keys as part of the required output contract.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-CONFIG
          7.5. Configured Relation Classifier GHOST-PREPROCESS-CLASSIFIER-CONFIG The current relation classifier shall be represented by a versioned Agent Stack configuration defining:
          - classifier identity;
          - model settings;
          - static classifier rules;
          - ActiveBrief and current-prompt bindings;
          - the two decision targets; and
          - the structured output mapping.

          The functional behavior in this file shall remain independent of the physical JSON path.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION
            7.1. Two Independent Classification Dimensions GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION The relation classifier shall return exactly two semantic decisions:
            - prompt_materiality; and
            - topic_relation.

            The classifier shall not collapse both dimensions into one combined LLM label.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
              1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

              The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

              The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
              • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
                2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CLASSIFIER-VALUES
    12.14. AC: Exact Relation Classifier Values GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-CLASSIFIER-VALUES Given a normal relation-classifier call, the accepted parsed result shall contain one allowed prompt_materiality value and one allowed topic_relation value.

    Any other value shall fail explicitly before routing action fields are written as a valid decision.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT
      7.9. Deterministic Routing Decision Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT GHOST shall deterministically derive one routing decision containing:
      - prompt_materiality;
      - topic_relation;
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval;
      - final_prompt_mode;
      - allow_memory_context; and
      - memory_context_policy.

      The LLM shall not populate these action fields directly.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION
        7.1. Two Independent Classification Dimensions GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION The relation classifier shall return exactly two semantic decisions:
        - prompt_materiality; and
        - topic_relation.

        The classifier shall not collapse both dimensions into one combined LLM label.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SIX-STATE-MATRIX
    12.15. AC: Six-State Deterministic Routing Matrix GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SIX-STATE-MATRIX Given each valid STRONG/WEAK and SAME_TOPIC/RELATED_DOMAIN/IRRELEVANT combination, GHOST shall produce exactly the routing values defined in the corresponding state requirement.

    The same classification pair shall always produce the same routing decision.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-SAME
      7.12. Routing State: Weak and Same Topic GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-SAME For WEAK + SAME_TOPIC, GHOST shall set:
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = true;
      - final_prompt_mode = full_activebrief;
      - allow_memory_context = true;
      - memory_context_policy = allowed.

      The effective document query shall contain the current prompt and the full ActiveBrief body.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT
        7.9. Deterministic Routing Decision Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT GHOST shall deterministically derive one routing decision containing:
        - prompt_materiality;
        - topic_relation;
        - use_activebrief_for_retrieval;
        - final_prompt_mode;
        - allow_memory_context; and
        - memory_context_policy.

        The LLM shall not populate these action fields directly.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION
          7.1. Two Independent Classification Dimensions GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION The relation classifier shall return exactly two semantic decisions:
          - prompt_materiality; and
          - topic_relation.

          The classifier shall not collapse both dimensions into one combined LLM label.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-SAME-QUERY
    12.16. AC: Weak Same-Topic Retrieval Query GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-SAME-QUERY Given WEAK + SAME_TOPIC and a non-empty ActiveBrief body, the finalized effective retrieval query shall include the current TASK/PURPOSE/CONTEXT projection and the full ActiveBrief body.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-RELATED
      7.14. Routing State: Weak and Related Domain GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-RELATED For WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN, GHOST shall set:
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = true;
      - final_prompt_mode = title_related_domain_note;
      - allow_memory_context = true;
      - memory_context_policy = allowed_related_domain.

      The effective document query shall contain the current prompt and the ActiveBrief title, but not the full ActiveBrief body.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT
        7.9. Deterministic Routing Decision Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT GHOST shall deterministically derive one routing decision containing:
        - prompt_materiality;
        - topic_relation;
        - use_activebrief_for_retrieval;
        - final_prompt_mode;
        - allow_memory_context; and
        - memory_context_policy.

        The LLM shall not populate these action fields directly.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION
          7.1. Two Independent Classification Dimensions GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION The relation classifier shall return exactly two semantic decisions:
          - prompt_materiality; and
          - topic_relation.

          The classifier shall not collapse both dimensions into one combined LLM label.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-RELATED-QUERY
    12.17. AC: Weak Related-Domain Retrieval Query GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-RELATED-QUERY Given WEAK + RELATED_DOMAIN and a non-empty ActiveBrief title, the finalized effective retrieval query shall include the current TASK/PURPOSE/CONTEXT projection and the ActiveBrief title without the full ActiveBrief body.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-IRRELEVANT
      7.16. Routing State: Weak and Irrelevant GHOST-PREPROCESS-STATE-WEAK-IRRELEVANT For WEAK + IRRELEVANT, GHOST shall set:
      - use_activebrief_for_retrieval = false;
      - final_prompt_mode = none;
      - allow_memory_context = false;
      - memory_context_policy = disabled.

      The effective document-retrieval query shall be empty, signaling that document retrieval is not applicable for this state.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT
        7.9. Deterministic Routing Decision Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT GHOST shall deterministically derive one routing decision containing:
        - prompt_materiality;
        - topic_relation;
        - use_activebrief_for_retrieval;
        - final_prompt_mode;
        - allow_memory_context; and
        - memory_context_policy.

        The LLM shall not populate these action fields directly.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION
          7.1. Two Independent Classification Dimensions GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION The relation classifier shall return exactly two semantic decisions:
          - prompt_materiality; and
          - topic_relation.

          The classifier shall not collapse both dimensions into one combined LLM label.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-IRRELEVANT-QUERY
    12.18. AC: Weak Irrelevant Retrieval Suppression GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-WEAK-IRRELEVANT-QUERY Given WEAK + IRRELEVANT, the finalized effective document-retrieval query shall be empty and memory context shall be disabled.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
      9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

      The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
        1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

        The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

        The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD
    12.19. AC: Query Rebuilt after Classification GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-QUERY-REBUILD Given an initial pre-classification query and a final routing state that changes ActiveBrief contribution or suppresses retrieval, the effective retrieval-query field shall be rebuilt after classification and shall reflect the final state at handoff.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-FINAL-MODE-AUTHORITY
      7.17. Final Prompt Mode Authority GHOST-PREPROCESS-FINAL-MODE-AUTHORITY Central prompt projection shall honor the deterministic final_prompt_mode stored by prompt preparation.

      The projector shall not independently derive an ActiveBrief inclusion behavior that contradicts the routing decision.

      The supported current modes shall mean:
      - full_activebrief: include title and full brief as supporting continuity context;
      - title_related_domain_note: include the title with related-domain continuity framing;
      - title_topic_shift_note: include the title only for explicit topic-shift awareness;
      - none: include no ActiveBrief content.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT
        7.9. Deterministic Routing Decision Contract GHOST-PREPROCESS-DECISION-CONTRACT GHOST shall deterministically derive one routing decision containing:
        - prompt_materiality;
        - topic_relation;
        - use_activebrief_for_retrieval;
        - final_prompt_mode;
        - allow_memory_context; and
        - memory_context_policy.

        The LLM shall not populate these action fields directly.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION
          7.1. Two Independent Classification Dimensions GHOST-PREPROCESS-TWO-DIMENSION-CLASSIFICATION The relation classifier shall return exactly two semantic decisions:
          - prompt_materiality; and
          - topic_relation.

          The classifier shall not collapse both dimensions into one combined LLM label.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-FINAL-MODE
    12.20. AC: Final Prompt Mode Projection GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-FINAL-MODE Given a deterministic final_prompt_mode, the central projector shall include exactly the permitted ActiveBrief continuity material for that mode and shall not independently apply a contradictory state table.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL
      8.9. A2 Invalid Identifier Removal GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INVALID-REMOVAL For each A2 field, deterministic sanitization shall remove option identifiers that are not present in that field's allowed catalog.

      This rule shall remove invented identifiers, malformed identifiers, and identifiers copied from another field's catalog.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
        8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

        The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
          8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
          1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
          2. compose the selector messages;
          3. call the configured LLM path;
          4. parse the structured result; and
          5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
            8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

            The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
              8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

              Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
              • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
                1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

                The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

                The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID
    12.21. AC: A2 Invalid Identifier Sanitization GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-INVALID Given an A2 result containing an invented identifier or an identifier from another field's catalog, the sanitizer shall remove that identifier before SuperPrompt write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE
      8.12. Preserve Existing Value after Empty Sanitization GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-EMPTY-PRESERVE If an A2 field has no valid identifier after sanitization, A2 shall omit that field from write-back.

      A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value and shall not apply a catalog default as semantic repair after invalid LLM output.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
        8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

        The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
          8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
          1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
          2. compose the selector messages;
          3. call the configured LLM path;
          4. parse the structured result; and
          5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
            8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

            The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE
    12.22. AC: A2 Empty Sanitized Field Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-PRESERVE Given an A2 field whose candidate output becomes empty after sanitization, A2 shall preserve the existing preprocessing value by omitting that field from write-back.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE
      8.6. A2 Deterministic-Default Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-DEFAULT-MODE When A2 LLM mode is disabled, A2 shall construct its candidate selector result deterministically from configured defaults.

      If a configured default is absent, A2 may use the first allowed configured option or the allowed prefix required by a multi-selection target.

      The same deterministic sanitization and write-back rules shall apply after candidate construction.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
        8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

        The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
          8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

          Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT
    12.23. AC: A2 Deterministic-Default Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-DEFAULT Given use_llm = false and valid configured defaults, A2 shall produce a sanitized selector result without an LLM call, mark a2_llm_used false, and mark its mode as deterministic_defaults.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
      8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

      When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

      For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
        8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

        The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE
          8.5. A2 LLM Selector Mode GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-LLM-MODE When A2 LLM mode is enabled, A2 shall:
          1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
          2. compose the selector messages;
          3. call the configured LLM path;
          4. parse the structured result; and
          5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG
            8.2. Versioned A2 Configuration GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-CONFIG A2 shall obtain its prompt contract and option catalogs from a versioned Agent Stack configuration.

            The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.
            • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE
              8.1. A2 Input State GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-INPUT-STATE A2 shall operate on a SuperPrompt that has completed deterministic preprocessing and ActiveBrief routing.

              Its semantic input shall be derived from the current TASK, PURPOSE, and CONTEXT rather than from retrieved document chunks or synthesized memory context.

                1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

                The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL
    12.24. AC: A2 Identifier and Label Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-ID-LABEL Given valid sanitized A2 identifiers with configured labels, SuperPrompt extras shall retain the identifiers while the body fields contain their human-readable labels.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE
      8.16. A2 Stage Mark GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STAGE After successful A2 processing, SuperPrompt.stage shall be set to "a2".
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK
        8.15. A2 Body Write-Back GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-BODY-WRITEBACK A2 shall update only active target fields that retain valid sanitized values.

        A2 shall not overwrite TASK, PURPOSE, CONTEXT, FORMAT, TEXT, document context, memory context, or ActiveBrief routing metadata as a side effect of meta-field selection.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION
          8.13. A2 Identifier and Display-Label Separation GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-ID-LABEL-SEPARATION A2 shall preserve selected option identifiers in SuperPrompt extras for traceability.

          When a configured display label exists, the corresponding human-readable label shall be written into the SuperPrompt body field.

          For multi-selected system roles, the body value shall contain the ordered selected labels in a readable joined form.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT
            8.8. A2 Structured Selector Output GHOST-PREPROCESS-A2-STRUCTURED-OUTPUT A2 shall request and parse a JSON object using only configured output keys and allowed option identifiers.

            The model output shall not be written directly into SuperPrompt before deterministic sanitization.

              1. obtain the configured AgentPrompt;
              2. compose the selector messages;
              3. call the configured LLM path;
              4. parse the structured result; and
              5. apply deterministic selector sanitization before SuperPrompt write-back.

                The configuration shall define the A2 identity, selector mode, model settings, static decision rules, dynamic bindings, decision targets, output mappings, and allowed option catalogs.

    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE
    12.25. AC: A2 Lifecycle and Artifact Preservation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-A2-STATE After successful A2 execution, stage shall equal a2, history shall contain the new a2 entry, prompt_ready shall reflect the A2 selections, and existing document or memory retrieval artifacts shall remain unchanged.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY
      9.7. Prepared-State Consistency GHOST-PREPROCESS-HANDOFF-CONSISTENCY At handoff, the following shall describe the same current request and routing decision:
      - SuperPrompt body fields;
      - ActiveBrief relation extras;
      - deterministic routing decision;
      - effective retrieval-query text;
      - A2 selected identifiers and display labels;
      - lifecycle stage and history;
      - prompt_ready.

      Contradictory representations shall be treated as a defect.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
        9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

        The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF
    12.26. AC: Prepared Retrieval Handoff GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-HANDOFF At completion of prompt preparation, the SuperPrompt body, routing extras, effective retrieval query, A2 metadata, stage/history, and prompt_ready shall be mutually consistent and sufficient for Knowledge and Memory Retrieval to begin without reparsing the raw user prompt.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION
      9.8. No Knowledge Retrieval Execution GHOST-PREPROCESS-NO-KNOWLEDGE-EXECUTION The prompt-preparation capability shall not query ChromaDB, SPLADE indexes, document manifests, rerankers, A3, or A4.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF
        9.4. Knowledge Retrieval Handoff GHOST-PREPROCESS-KNOWLEDGE-HANDOFF The Knowledge Management path shall receive:
        - the prepared SuperPrompt;
        - the synchronized effective retrieval-query text; and
        - routing state sufficient to determine whether document retrieval is applicable.

        This handoff shall not precompute document candidates.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION
          9.1. Post-Classification Retrieval-Query Finalization GHOST-PREPROCESS-QUERY-FINALIZATION After ActiveBrief relation classification and deterministic routing, GHOST shall rebuild SuperPrompt.effective_retrieval_query_text from the final routing state.

          The query stored for downstream document retrieval shall not remain an earlier pre-classification projection when the final state requires ActiveBrief inclusion or retrieval suppression.
          • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
            1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

            The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

            The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL
    12.27. AC: No Retrieval inside Prompt Preparation GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-NO-RETRIEVAL Executing the complete prompt-preparation capability shall not query document or memory vector stores and shall not run reranking, A3, A4, or memory synthesis.
    • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION
      11.3. Requirement and Implementation Synchronization GHOST-PREPROCESS-CHANGE-SYNCHRONIZATION An approved change to prompt schema semantics, deterministic parsing, canonical field mapping, ActiveBrief routing, A2 targets, query projection, public interfaces, or SuperPrompt write-back shall update the affected requirements and verification assets together with the implementation change.

      Private refactoring that preserves required behavior and interfaces shall not require new functional requirements.

      Code that contradicts an approved requirement in this file shall be treated as a defect or unapproved change until the contradiction is resolved.
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS
        11.1. Prompt Function Code Trace Anchors GHOST-PREPROCESS-CODE-TRACE-ANCHORS Prompt-preparation requirements shall remain traceable to the modules and configurations that implement schema loading, deterministic parsing, name matching, ActiveBrief relation classification, A2 shaping, SuperPrompt mutation, query projection, and controller coordination.

        A private helper shall not become the owner of a system requirement merely because it currently contains the implementing function.
        • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT
          1.1. Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT GHOST shall provide one prompt-preparation capability that transforms raw user input into a structured and routed SuperPrompt ready for downstream Knowledge Management and Memory Management processing.

          The capability shall include deterministic preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, deterministic routing decisions, A2 PromptShaper execution, synchronized retrieval-query construction, and final prompt-preview projection.

          The capability shall stop at the handoff boundary and shall not execute document retrieval, memory retrieval, document reranking, evidence selection, evidence condensation, or memory-context synthesis.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION
    12.28. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization GHOST-PREPROCESS-AC-SYNCHRONIZATION An implementation baseline shall not be accepted when its prompt-schema mapping, routing table, query projection, final prompt projection, or A2 sanitization contradicts an approved requirement in this file.

    The contradiction shall be resolved by correcting the defect or approving a coordinated requirement and implementation change.
    Built with StrictDoc 0.25.0
